Job.JobTitle: Assistant Baseball Athletic Coach | West Forsyth High School
Job.JobDescription:
West Forsyth High School is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Assistant Coach: Baseball
This is a part-time coaching position only. Assistant baseball coaching supplement is based on the athletic special supplement schedule for 3.5 months in accordance with coaching experience. Supplement range for assistant baseball coach is $311.00 to $584.00 monthly.
Additional coaching duties (if any) with additional supplement pay will be assigned by the Principal and/or Athletic Director.
After applying, please also send resume to Mike Pennington, Athletics Director, West Forsyth High School, 1735 Lewisville Clemmons Road, Clemmons, NC 27012, mpennington@wsfcs.k12.nc.us
Job Qualifications: Successful high school and/or college baseball experience, or other relevant experience. Hold or be eligible to hold a NC commercial drivers license to operate a school activity bus preferred. Will be subject to periodic US Dept. of Transportation random controlled substance screenings.
When applying, please include all prior experience.
Position Type: Part-Time Salary: $311 to $584 Per Month Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching
Job Requirements - Citizenship, residency or work visa required

Lexington Senior High School is looking for a Softball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Athletic Coach
The coach should understand his/her sport and be familiar with all rules, courtesies, and procedures of the activity. The coach should know his/her students and be an effective leader. He/she should be concerned with winning gracefully and, when losing, accept the loss with poise and a realization that an educational experience probably has been achieved. Specific Duties 1. Teach the physical skills needed to play specific sport. 2. Know and follow all rules and regulations governing the s 3. Provide fair and equal opportunities for all candidate 4. Plan and supervise practice sessions in keeping with the age and maturity of the play 5. Meet with athletic director to prepare a tentative schedule of game 6. Establish rules for the proper use and care of athletic equipmen 7. Prepare and present to the athletic director a list of students desiring to participate so that eligibility can be determ 8. Check and monitor team members' academic 9. Maintain an inventory of all equipment and supplies. 10. Submit to the athletic director at the end of the season a list of any equipment and/or uniforms needed for the upcoming y 11. Submit a budget request to athletic director 12. Collect uniforms and equipment at the end of the sport season and account for same. (Plus more duties related to safety, communication, etc.) Job Requirements None specified

Job.JobTitle: Middle School-Assistant Track and Field Coach
Job.JobDescription:
The Middle School-Assistant Track and Field Coach role at Williston Middle School Central Campus is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Middle School-Assistant Track and Field Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Williston Middle School Central Campus, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Middle School-Assistant Track and Field Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Williston Middle School Central Campus, the Middle School-Assistant Track and Field Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Middle School-Assistant Track and Field Coach at Williston Middle School Central Campus is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

The High School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach position at Williston High School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The High School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Williston High School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The High School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The High School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the High School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach role at Williston High School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

The Middle School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach at Williston Middle School Central Campus is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Middle School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Williston Middle School Central Campus, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Middle School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Middle School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Williston Middle School Central Campus operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Williston Middle School Central Campus with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Middle School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Middle School-Girls Assistant Tennis Coach role at Williston Middle School Central Campus.

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Girls Soccer Coach (2 Positions Available)
Job.JobDescription:
The Assistant Girls Soccer Coach (2 Positions Available) position at Jamestown High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Assistant Girls Soccer Coach (2 Positions Available) works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Assistant Girls Soccer Coach (2 Positions Available) at Jamestown High School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Jamestown High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Assistant Girls Soccer Coach (2 Positions Available) encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Assistant Girls Soccer Coach (2 Positions Available) is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Assistant Girls Soccer Coach (2 Positions Available) position at Jamestown High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Job.JobTitle: Middle School Assistant Girls Track Coach
Job.JobDescription:
The Middle School Assistant Girls Track Coach at Jamestown Middle School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Middle School Assistant Girls Track Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Jamestown Middle School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Middle School Assistant Girls Track Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Middle School Assistant Girls Track Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Middle School Assistant Girls Track Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Jamestown Middle School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Manchester School Dist. is looking for a Baseball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Assumes responsibility for the entire program which includes: 1. Coaching and supervising the program. 2. Implements district athletic department policies. 3. Works with the MSD Athletic Director and Principal 4. Communicating school game schedule. 5. Communication of travel for out-of-town trips. 6. Verification of student physical examination/family ID requirements of the high school. 7. Remains with students and supervises them while traveling to away competitions. 8. Cares for athletes and injured athletes. 9. Cares for, maintains and oversees the storage of all equipment and uniforms. 10. Maintains a positive staff relationship with other coaches. 11. Performs other duties as assigned by the MSD Athletic Director and Principal 12. Communicate with parents to inform them about the program as well as your expectations of them and the players. 13. Leads by example 14. Apply and enforce student discipline during athletic contests, practice sessions, and while on trips off school property in accordance with Student Code of Conduct and student handbook.
Position Type: Part-Time Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching
Job Requirements Citizenship, residency or work visa required
Job.Location: Southside Middle School - Manchester, New Hampshire

Job.JobDescription:
Highland Goffe's Falls Elementary is looking for a Basketball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Elementary Boys Basketball Coach position at Highland Goffe's Falls Elementary provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Elementary Boys Basketball Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Highland Goffe's Falls Elementary, the Elementary Boys Basketball Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Elementary Boys Basketball Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Elementary Boys Basketball Coach at Highland Goffe's Falls Elementary is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.Location: Highland Goffe's Falls Elementary - Manchester, New Hampshire

1. Implements district athletic department policies. 2. Communicating the game schedule. 3. Verification of student physical examination/Family ID requirements of the high school. 4. Remains with students and supervises them while traveling to away competitions. 5. Cares for athletes and injured athletes. 6. Cares for, maintains and oversees the storage of all equipment and uniforms. 7. Maintains a positive staff relationship with other coaches. 8. Performs other duties as assigned by the MSD Athletic Director/Athletic Coordinator and Principal. 9. Leads by example regarding promotion of sportsmanship. 10. Promotes the entire program by encouraging participation of players of the upcoming season. 11. Job.JobTitle: Boys Swimming and Diving - Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Plymouth-Canton Community Schools is looking for a Swimming and Diving Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: 1. High school graduate or equivalent 2. Applicants must have experience in high school level coaching or playing at college or university level in sport to which they are applying. 3. Applicant must have a time schedule compatible with that of the high school. 4. CPR and/or first aide training preferred. 5. Demonstrated ability to work with parents and children.
REPORTS TO: Assistant Principal of Activities and Athletics RATE OF PAY: According to the P-CEA Master Agreement - Schedule "B" BEGINNING DATE: 2025/2026 Winter Season
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. The assistant coach will be responsible for the entire organization of the sport to which he/she is assigned. 2. The assistant coach will be directly responsible for all equipment in his/her sport. A complete inventory of all equipment will be filed with the director at the end of each season. 3. The assistant coach will be responsible for all awards presented in his/her sport and a season summary. 4. Bending, lifting, and overhead work required. 5. Performs other tasks as assigned.
This description is intended to describe the essential job functions, the general supplemental functions and the essential requirements for the performance of this job. It is not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and requirements of a person so classified. Other functions may be assigned and management retains the right to add or change the duties at any time.
EVALUATION: Performance of this position will be evaluated annually.
METHOD OF APPLICATION: Internal and external applicants should complete the online application in the Frontline system. External applicants will be hired and paid by EDUStaff. www.EDUStaff.org
DEADLINE FOR APPLICATION: Open until filled
Job.SalaryRange: According to the P-CEA Master Agreement - Schedule "B"

Job.JobTitle: Middle School Volleyball Coach (8th Grade) - Mason
Job.JobDescription:
The Middle School Volleyball Coach (8th Grade) - Mason at Waterford School District is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Middle School Volleyball Coach (8th Grade) - Mason are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Waterford School District use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Middle School Volleyball Coach (8th Grade) - Mason recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Middle School Volleyball Coach (8th Grade) - Mason collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Middle School Volleyball Coach (8th Grade) - Mason maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Waterford School District. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

The Varsity Boys Soccer Head Coach - Mott encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Varsity Boys Soccer Head Coach - Mott at Waterford School District is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

The Varsity Football Coach - Kettering role at Waterford School District is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Varsity Football Coach - Kettering is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Waterford School District, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Varsity Football Coach - Kettering sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Waterford School District, the Varsity Football Coach - Kettering is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Varsity Football Coach - Kettering at Waterford School District is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

North Farmington High School is looking for a Lacrosse Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach at North Farmington High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At North Farmington High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach at North Farmington High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. In practical terms, the 25-ATH-33 Middle School Assistant Track Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. Job.JobTitle: Assistant Sponsor- JV Cheer Fall and Winter for 2026-2027
Job.JobDescription:
Wentzville R-IV is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Assistant JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Sponsor- JV Cheer Fall and Winter for 2026 position at Wentzville R-IV School District is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Assistant Sponsor- JV Cheer Fall and Winter for 2026 works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Assistant Sponsor- JV Cheer Fall and Winter for 2026 at Wentzville R-IV School District sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Wentzville R-IV School District, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Assistant Sponsor- JV Cheer Fall and Winter for 2026 encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Assistant Sponsor- JV Cheer Fall and Winter for 2026 is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Assistant Sponsor- JV Cheer Fall and Winter for 2026 position at Wentzville R-IV School District is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.SalaryRange: starts at step 1 $2,338.00 each, for both fall and winter seasons, 5% of the extra duty salary schedule

Job.JobDescription:
04 D'Iberville Middle is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer coach to join our program. Apply now.
QUALIFICATIONS 1. All instructional personnel shall be certified and licensed in accordance with state law and the regulations of the Mississippi Department of Education. Such licensure and transcripts of credits shall be on file in the office of the Superintendent of Education. 2. In accordance with Mississippi Code S37-9-17, all personnel employed by the public-school system shall be required to pass a criminal background check and a current child abuse registry check. 3. A satisfactory employment record. REPORTS TO: Building Principal, Assistant Principal, and or Directors JOB GOAL: To help students learn subject matter and skills that will contribute to their development as mature, able, and responsible men and women. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Such duties and responsibilities as may be assigned, including but not limited to: 1. To abide by the standards of conduct as defined by the Mississippi Educator Code of Ethics. 2. To plan, organize and conduct a program of instruction based on the Mississippi Standards/Frameworks. 3. To assume custody of the pupils assigned to their care and to take precautions that will ensure the pupil's safety and general welfare. 4. To participate in all pre-school conferences and in-service training programs, unless assigned school duty conflicts. 5. To perform extra-curricular duties assigned by the principal, such as room, hall, playground and similar essential duties. 6. Coordinates with other professional staff members, especially within grade level/department, and participates in faculty meetings and committees. 7. To keep the classes in session each day during school hours and not dismiss them for any time without the prior consent of the principal. 8. To be responsible for all school property and equipment entrusted to them. 9. Ensures that student conduct conforms to the school's standards and school district policies, and establishes and maintains standards of student behavior needed to achieve a functional learning atmosphere in the classroom. 10. Organizes and maintains a system for accurate and complete record-keeping, grading, and reporting for all student activities, achievement and attendance as required by district procedures and applicable laws. 11. To make any and all requests to the School Board through the principal and superintendent. 12. To be strictly accountable to the principal and superintendent for the performance of their teaching duties. 13. Develops lesson plans and instructional materials and translates lesson plans into learning experiences to develop pertinent sequential assignments, challenge students, and best utilize the available time for instruction. 14. Conducts ongoing assessment of student learning and progress, and modifies instructional methods to fit individual student's needs, including students with special needs. 15. In all cases where a teacher terminates employment during the school year, to provide lesson plans for at least two weeks in advance for the replacement teacher. 16. Promotes a classroom environment that is safe and conducive to individualized and small group instruction, and student learning. 17. To notify the principal or his/her designee directly upon any anticipated absences, obtaining prior approval when required. 18. Encourages parental involvement in student's education and ensures effective communication with students and parents. 19. To abide by all reasonable rules and assignments set down by the principal and other administrative personnel. 20. To avoid doctrinal, sectarian, and denominational teaching. 21. Continues to acquire professional knowledge and learn of current developments in the educational field by attending seminars, workshops or professional meetings, or by conducting research. 22. To strive at all times to maintain cordial conferences with the home and parents, keeping the parents informed of the student's progress and holding conferences whenever necessary. 23. To be thoroughly familiar with and actively support the enforcement of all Board of Trustees' policies and procedures, rules and regulations of the Superintendent of Education, and rules set forth by the principal and in the school handbook(s) 24. Remain alert at all times to hazards including, but not limited to the following: obstructed walkways, unsafe playground equipment, unauthorized personnel on school properties, unsafe floor surfaces, and any other unsafe conditions. 25. Any other duties within the scope of this job description as assigned by the Principal, Assistant Principal, and/or Director. ADDITIONAL DUTIES

Job.JobDescription:
The Assistant Track Coach at Concord High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Assistant Track Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Concord High School, the Assistant Track Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Assistant Track Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Concord High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Assistant Track Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Job.JobTitle: Head Coach Outdoor Track - Lewiston Middle School
Job.JobDescription:
The Head Coach Outdoor Track - Lewiston Middle School at Lewiston Middle School is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Head Coach Outdoor Track - Lewiston Middle School are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Lewiston Middle School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Head Coach Outdoor Track - Lewiston Middle School recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Head Coach Outdoor Track - Lewiston Middle School collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Head Coach Outdoor Track - Lewiston Middle School maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Lewiston Middle School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: Middle School Boys Lacrosse Coach - SPMS
Job.JobDescription:
The Middle School Boys Lacrosse Coach - SPMS position at S. Portland Public Sch. provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Middle School Boys Lacrosse Coach - SPMS prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At S. Portland Public Sch., the Middle School Boys Lacrosse Coach - SPMS works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Middle School Boys Lacrosse Coach - SPMS encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Middle School Boys Lacrosse Coach - SPMS at S. Portland Public Sch. is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Job.JobTitle: Head Wrestling Coach - Massabesic Middle School
Job.JobDescription:
Massabesic Middle School is looking for a Wrestling Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Type: Athletics/Activities/Coach
Date Posted: 12/17/2025
Location: Massabesic Middle School
Regional School Unit 57
Position Title: Head Wrestling Coach Reports To: High School Athletic Director, in conjunction with the Principal
Qualifications
Knowledge and background in the sport of wrestling. Meets RSU 57 eligibility requirements, including: First Aid Certification CPR Certification Concussion Management Course Heat Acclimatization Course RSU 57 Background Check Maine Department of Education Criminal History Record Check
All requirements must be met within timelines specified by RSU 57. *
Position Summary
The Head Wrestling Coach is responsible for the planning, organization, supervision, and evaluation of the high school wrestling program. The Head Coach instructs student-athletes in fundamental skills, strategy, and physical conditioning necessary to achieve individual and team success while supporting the mission and athletic philosophy of RSU 57.
Essential Duties and Responsibilities
Organize and supervise all aspects of the high school wrestling program. Demonstrate thorough knowledge of local, state, and school athletic policies and procedures. Implement and enforce athletic department policies and team rules. Attend all required meetings as scheduled by the school, Athletic Director, and Maine Principals' Association (MPA). Emphasize the importance of academics, sportsmanship, citizenship, and character development as part of the athletic philosophy. Maintain high standards for athlete performance and conduct. Model appropriate behavior, professionalism, and sportsmanship at all times. Dress appropriately for practices, competitions, and meetings. Apply sound knowledge of the sport, including training and conditioning techniques. Seek opportunities for professional growth and incorporate new techniques and ideas when appropriate. Teach skills that are developmentally appropriate and safe for individual student-athletes. Supervise student-athletes at all times to ensure safety and accountability. Maintain, inventory, store, and care for athletic equipment and uniforms. Inspect equipment and facilities regularly to ensure safe conditions. Prepare for and supervise all scheduled practices, competitions, and events. Establish, communicate, and enforce appropriate team rules and expectations. Utilize appropriate emergency procedures and injury response protocols. Encourage leadership and positive behavior among student-athletes on and off the mat. Communicate promptly, professionally, and appropriately with parents and guardians. Participate in preseason informational meetings with parents/guardians and athletes. Interact effectively and appropriately with officials, opponents, and event personnel. Meet all deadlines for paperwork and related duties. Maintain required records and documentation for student-athletes. Perform other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or Principal.
Language and Communication Skills
Ability to read and interpret documents relevant to the position, including athletic policies, handbooks, and state guidelines. Ability to communicate clearly, concisely, and correctly in both oral and written form with parents, athletes, coaches, administrators, and officials. Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with administrators, coaching staff, athletes, parents, and the community.
Mathematical Skills
Basic understanding of budgeting, inventory management, and record-keeping practices.
Physical Demands
While performing the essential duties of this position, the employee is regularly required to stand, walk, talk, and hear. The position frequently requires physical demonstration of skills, including running, jumping, throwing, grappling techniques, and general body movement. The employee may be required to lift and carry items weighing more than 20 pounds. Occasional prolonged sitting and frequent travel by bus are required. Extended hours may be necessary to meet the demands of the position.
Work Environment
The work environment noise level is usually moderate but may occasionally be excessive. Work is performed both indoors and outdoors and may involve exposure to varying weather conditions.
Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions of the position.

The Softball Head Coach - 7th Grade role at Massabesic Middle School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Softball Head Coach - 7th Grade is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Massabesic Middle School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Softball Head Coach - 7th Grade sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Massabesic Middle School, the Softball Head Coach - 7th Grade is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Softball Head Coach - 7th Grade at Massabesic Middle School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

The Softball Head Coach - 8th Grade role at Massabesic Middle School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Softball Head Coach - 8th Grade is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Massabesic Middle School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Softball Head Coach - 8th Grade listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Softball Head Coach - 8th Grade encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Softball Head Coach - 8th Grade follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Massabesic Middle School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Softball Head Coach - 8th Grade at Massabesic Middle School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

The 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach at North Farmington High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. The 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach at North Farmington High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobDescription:
The Cross Country Assistant Coach ~ 2 Openings at Francis Howell Central High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Cross Country Assistant Coach ~ 2 Openings is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Francis Howell Central High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Cross Country Assistant Coach ~ 2 Openings helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Cross Country Assistant Coach ~ 2 Openings works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Francis Howell Central High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Francis Howell Central High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Cross Country Assistant Coach ~ 2 Openings reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Cross Country Assistant Coach ~ 2 Openings role at Francis Howell Central High School.

Coach at Francis Howell North High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: Asst. Tennis Coach - Sentinel High School
Job.JobDescription:
The Asst. Tennis Coach - Sentinel High School at Sentinel High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Asst. Tennis Coach - Sentinel High School manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Sentinel High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Asst. Tennis Coach - Sentinel High School demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Asst. Tennis Coach - Sentinel High School follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Asst. Tennis Coach - Sentinel High School at Sentinel High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

7/8 Boys Basketball Coaches (multiple positions) at SMS or CJMS

Description: Bozeman middle schools have one or more girls' basketball coaching positions open for the upcoming season. Prior coaching experience is preferred. Prior playing experience at high school and/or collegiate level is desired. Applicants must complete the National Federation of High Schools online coaching course - Concussion in Sports. Job.JobDescription:
The Assistant High School Tennis Coach role at High School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Assistant High School Tennis Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At High School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Assistant High School Tennis Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At High School, the Assistant High School Tennis Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Assistant High School Tennis Coach at High School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

The High School Assistant Volleyball Coach position at High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the High School Assistant Volleyball Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At High School, the High School Assistant Volleyball Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The High School Assistant Volleyball Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the High School Assistant Volleyball Coach at High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
