Job.JobDescription:
The COACH - HEAD COACH - BASEBALL position at Guilford County is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the COACH - HEAD COACH - BASEBALL works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The COACH - HEAD COACH - BASEBALL at Guilford County sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Guilford County, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The COACH - HEAD COACH - BASEBALL encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The COACH - HEAD COACH - BASEBALL is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the COACH - HEAD COACH - BASEBALL position at Guilford County is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

The Head Football Coach at North Iredell High School is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Head Football Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at North Iredell High School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Head Football Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Head Football Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Head Football Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at North Iredell High School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

The Assistant Football Coach at Chatham County is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Assistant Football Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Chatham County reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Assistant Football Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Assistant Football Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Assistant Football Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Chatham County. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Mattamuskeet School is looking for a Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
JOB GOAL: 1. To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategies, and physical conditioning necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. 2. The success of athletic programs has a strong influence on the community's image of the entire school system. The public exposure is a considerable responsibility, and community/parent pressure for winning performance is taxing, but must not override the objectives of good sportsmanship and good mental health for student participants.
EXPERIENCE: Has previous experience as an effective coach in the assigned sport.
PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Hyde County Schools Athletic Department, and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of his/her sport program. 2. Conduct themselves in an appropriate professional manner, serving as a role model for the student athletes. 3. Has knowledge of the existing systems, state and league regulations; implements the same consistently, and interprets them for his/her staff. 4. Understands the proper administrative line of command; refers all request or grievances through proper channels. 5. Must make sure all athletes are current on their physical by checking with Secretary/Nurse. The original must be returned to the secretary. A copy will be kept with the current nurse.
STAFF RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Establishes the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by the staff. 2. Trains and informs staff and encourages professional growth through clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. 3. Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation, and at season's end, analyzes staff effectiveness. 4. Maintains discipline, adjusts grievances and work to increase morale and cooperation. 5. Performs other duties which may be assigned by the Athletic Director.
PROGRAM RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Is fully responsible for the overall supervision of the program. This includes Middle School. 2. Is in charge of assignments and duties of assistant coaches working in the sport. 3. Is responsible for keeping practice periods for the sport within the confines of the time specified by the Athletic Director. 4. Is responsible for the general upkeep and protection of equipment under his/her jurisdiction. 5. Is responsible for a complete inventory of equipment used for the sport. 6. Is responsible for submitting a summary of the yearly program with all necessary reports for the end of the season data. 7. Is responsible for drafting and submitting a budget for the sport to the Athletic Director. 8. Is responsible to maintain preparing practice and game field/court for practice or games. 9. Is responsible for evaluation of all sub-varsity coaches. 10. Is responsible for having practice regularly and a minimum of 1 hour and 45 minutes. Middle School 1 hour and 30 minutes 11. Is responsible for working with all other coaches in sport and implementing their program from the ground up starting in middle school.
RESPONSIBILITIES TO STUDENTS: 1. Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. 2. By his/her presence at all practices, games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. 3. Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies. 4. Completes and submits proper AED and Concussion forms and reports immediately and maintains therein. 5. Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians. 6. Determines discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary, and contacts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. 7. Assists athletes in their college or advanced educational selection.
WEBSITE DUTIES: 1. The head coach is responsible for making sure the team's website page is updated with scores, pictures, roster, and etc. 2. Work with the Athletic Director in making sure the website is kept current.
WORKING CONDITIONS: The usual and customary methods of performing the job's functions require the following physical demands: some lifting, carrying, pushing, and/or pulling: and signification fine finger dexterity. Generally the job requires 20% sitting, 40% walking, and 40% standing.
REQUIRED TRAINING 1. NFHS, Fundamentals of coaching, Concussion in sports 2. Crash course concussion 3. Account with Dragonfly max 4. Current First Aid and CPR card
This job description in no way states or implies that these are the only duties to be performed by this employee. The Assistant Coach will be required to follow any other instructions and to perform any other related duties as assigned by the Head Coach, Athletic Director& Principal or appropriate administrator. Hyde County Schools reserves the right to update, revise or change this job description and related duties at any time.
Job.Location: Mattamuskeet School - Swan Quarter, North Carolina

Job.JobDescription:
Norfolk Senior High is looking for a Football Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
It is the policy of Norfolk Public Schools to not discriminate on the basis of sex, disability, race, color, religion, veteran status, national or ethnic origin, age, marital status, pregnancy, childbirth or related medical condition, or other protected status in its educational programs, admission policies, employment policies or other administered programs. Persons requiring accommodations to apply and/or be considered for positions are asked to make their request to the Superintendent.
JOB TITLE: ATHLETIC TRAINER - SENIOR HIGH
REQUIREMENTS: Bachelor of Science degree in a related field BOC certification Emergency Cardiac Care certification Licensed in the State of Nebraska Ability to pass background check
REPORTS TO: Athletic Director RECEIVES GUIDANCE FROM: Athletic Director
TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: 260 days
EVALUATION: Evaluated annually in accordance with provisions of the district’s Personnel Evaluation Policy
ESSENTIAL DUTIES: Consistent and punctual “on-site” attendance at the worksite is an essential job function Sports performance experience Exceptional interpersonal communication skills Proven ability to collaborate, effectively communicate, and work independently Outstanding customer service Mobility, strength, visual, and auditory acuity are adequate for immediate response to patients’ needs Evaluates injuries and develops treatment and exercise plans within the scope of practice, re-evaluates treatment plans, as needed, to ensure maximum patient benefit Documents patient treatment per professional and regulatory standards Provide athletic training services for all home athletic contests and away varsity football games. If a conflict arises between an away varsity football game and a home contest, the varsity football event will supersede. Act as liaison between family physicians and specialists, the school district, athletes, and their parents. Provide coverage at practices from the beginning of the fall sport season to the conclusion of the spring season, along with summer camps and activities as directed by the Athletic Director. Maintain a line of communication with the team physician(s) regarding athletic health care and recommended treatment/rehabilitation for all athletic injuries. Maintain an effective and efficient athletic training room. Maintain communication with parents on the care & treatment of their student-athlete. File all necessary reports associated with athletic injuries and/or incidents. Maintain a daily treatment log. Oversee the ordering of supplies and equipment for the athletic training room and maintain an up-to-date inventory. Provide the Athletic Director with an annual budget for supplies and equipment.
OTHER REQUIREMENTS (Intellectual, Sensory): Effective oral and written communication skills Effective leadership and organizational skills Decision-making skills Conflict management skills Public relations skills
Position Type: Full-Time Salary: $45,000 to $55,000 Per Year Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Trainer

High School is looking for a Volleyball Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Activities - Head Varsity Volleyball Coach role at High School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Activities - Head Varsity Volleyball Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At High School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Activities - Head Varsity Volleyball Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent High School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Activities - Head Varsity Volleyball Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Activities - Head Varsity Volleyball Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Activities - Head Varsity Volleyball Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at High School.

Job.JobTitle: Activities - Assistant Softball Coach (1 year only)
Job.JobDescription:
Fremont High School is looking for a Softball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Activities - Assistant Softball Coach (1 year only) at Fremont High School is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Activities - Assistant Softball Coach (1 year only) are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Fremont High School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Activities - Assistant Softball Coach (1 year only) recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Activities - Assistant Softball Coach (1 year only) collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Activities - Assistant Softball Coach (1 year only) maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Fremont High School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Central High School is looking for a Track & Field Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Assumes responsibility for the entire program which includes: 1. Coaching the varsity squad and supervising JV & assistant coaches within the program. 2. Giving guidance to the high school junior varsity program. 3. Implements district athletic department policies. 4. Assists in the selection and evaluation of Junior Varsity and assistant coaching staff. 5. Works with the MSD Athletic Director, Athletic Coordinator and Principal 6. Communicating high school game schedule. 7. Communication of travel for out-of-town trips. 8. Verification of student physical examination/Family ID requirements of the high school. 9. Remains with students and supervises them while traveling to away competitions. 10. Cares for athletes and injured athletes. 11. Cares for, maintains and oversees the storage of all equipment and uniforms. 12. Prepares award lists and season records. 13. Attends all state meetings where the program is involved and operates within the policies of the MSD. 14. Keeps informed on current trends in your sport and attends and participates in clinics in your sport. 15. Maintains active membership in related professional organizations. 16. Maintains a positive staff relationship with other coaches. 17. Performs other duties as assigned by the MSD Athletic Director/Athletic Coordinator and Principal to inform them about the program as well as your expectations of them and the players. 18. Communicates with players about personal development during the season as well as the off-season. 19. Holds meetings with parents regarding sportsmanship. 20. Promotes the high school program by encouraging participation of players of the upcoming season. 21. Apply and enforce student discipline during athletic contests, practice sessions, and while on trips off school property in accordance with Student Code of Conduct and student handbook. Citizenship, residency or work visa required

Job.JobTitle: Boys Middle School B Lacrosse Head Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Rundlett Middle School is looking for a Lacrosse Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Boys Middle School B Lacrosse Head Coach position at Rundlett Middle School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Boys Middle School B Lacrosse Head Coach is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Rundlett Middle School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Boys Middle School B Lacrosse Head Coach encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Boys Middle School B Lacrosse Head Coach also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Boys Middle School B Lacrosse Head Coach role at Rundlett Middle School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Job.JobTitle: Girls Middle School A Lacrosse Head Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Rundlett Middle School is looking for a Lacrosse Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Girls Middle School A Lacrosse Head Coach position at Rundlett Middle School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Girls Middle School A Lacrosse Head Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Rundlett Middle School, the Girls Middle School A Lacrosse Head Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Girls Middle School A Lacrosse Head Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Girls Middle School A Lacrosse Head Coach at Rundlett Middle School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Concord High School is looking for a Soccer Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Girls JV Soccer Head Coach position at Concord High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Girls JV Soccer Head Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Girls JV Soccer Head Coach at Concord High School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Concord High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Girls JV Soccer Head Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Girls JV Soccer Head Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Girls JV Soccer Head Coach position at Concord High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Hudson Memorial School is looking for a Softball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Girls Softball Coach role at Hudson Memorial School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Girls Softball Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Hudson Memorial School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Girls Softball Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Hudson Memorial School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Girls Softball Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Girls Softball Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Girls Softball Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Hudson Memorial School.

Job.JobTitle: JV Girls Lacrosse Coach | Windham high School | 2025-2026 School Year
Job.JobDescription:
Windham High School is looking for a Lacrosse Assistant JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Coach, Windham SAU #95 Windham, NH 2025-2026 School Year The purpose of these positions is to instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the students shall receive instruction that will lead to the formulation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, sportsmanship, and service to others. Job Summary: Windham High School is accepting applications for: Varsity Girls Lacrosse Qualifications: Ideal candidate(s) should have a minimum of three (3) years of successful experience in the respective sport as a head coach at or high school. OR at least five (5) years of successful experience as an assistant coach in college or high school is preferred. Secondary teaching certification is preferred but not required. Must have or be able to acquire annual CPR/First Aid Certification along with select NFHS and State of NH certifications, have a strong knowledge and commitment to following rules, expectations, policies, and procedures of the Windham School District and NHIAA. Salary: In accordance with the collective bargaining agreement of the WEA. Dissemination of Notice: The District does not discriminate on the basis of sex and prohibits sex discrimination in any education program or activity that it operates, as required by Title IX, including in admission and employment. Application Procedure: All applicants must apply using the district's online application system at Windham School District Administrative Unit 95. No phone calls, please. All candidates are responsible for providing evidence that they meet the above qualifications, which must be included with their letter of application. Position Type: Part-Time Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching Job Requirements At least 3 year(s) of relevant experience preferred. High School/Trade School degree preferred. Citizenship, residency or work visa required Contact Information Catherine Hennigar 64 London Bridge Road Windham, New Hampshire 03087 Phone: (603)845-1550 Email: chennigar@windhamsd.org
Job.Location: Windham High School - Windham, New Hampshire
Job.Experience: 3-5 years
Job.SalaryRange: In accordance with the collective bargaining agreement of the WEA

Job.JobDescription: st' id='p33425_724'> School Improvement Coordinator - Secondary JobID: 33425 Position Type: Administration/ School Improvement Date Posted: 2/25/2026 Location: Accelerated Learning Academy Closing Date: Until Filled District: Flint Community Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Job Summary: The School Improvement Coordinator works collaboratively with the building's school continuous improvement team and Principal to provide academic support for the teaching staff, implementing the best practices of instruction that utilize state and local assessment data. The School Improvement Coordinator is instrumental in shaping the educational environment, supporting staff, and fostering student achievement. Please see attached job description for a detail list of the essential functions for this position. Available Locations: Accelerated Learning Academy Salary & Benefits: Administrative Salary Schedule Starting Salary $83,440 & $5,000 sign on bonus 219 days per year Health, dental, vision, life and disability insurance. Employees shall be subject to the co-payment of health care costs equal to the difference between the premium cost and the annual cost limitation imposed on public employers. Qualifications: Education: Master's Degree in the field of Education is required. Must have a valid State of Michigan elementary teaching certificate for placement in an elementary school. State of Michigan School Administrator Certification required (see note below). *NOTE: Pursuant to MCL 380.1246 subsection (3), a person employed as a school administrator, after January 4, 2010, will have six months to enroll in a program leading to certification as a school administrator and three years to meet certification requirements. Per the School Administrator Certification Code, a person employed as a school administrator, whose primary responsibility is to administer instructional programs, must hold a valid Michigan School Administrator Certificate or work under a valid school Administrator Permit. Administrators not possessing a School Administrator Certificate at time of hire must obtain annual School Administrator permit and demonstrate progress toward certification. Experience: Minimum of 5 years of successful certified teaching experience at a level appropriate for corresponding building placement (elementary versus secondary) is required. Minimum of 2 years of experience in an educational leadership role is preferred. Minimum of 2 years of experience as an academic coach, instructional specialist, or curriculum coordinator is preferred. Experience in grant management and data interpretation and analysis preferred. Job.CompanyDescription: Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
The 7th Grade Assistant Football Coach at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the 7th Grade Assistant Football Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The 7th Grade Assistant Football Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the 7th Grade Assistant Football Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the 7th Grade Assistant Football Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.
Job.CompanyDescription: Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobTitle: 7Th Grade Volleyball - Head Coach (2 positions available)
Job.JobDescription:
The 7Th Grade Volleyball - Head Coach (2 positions available) position at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the 7Th Grade Volleyball - Head Coach (2 positions available) works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The 7Th Grade Volleyball - Head Coach (2 positions available) at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The 7Th Grade Volleyball - Head Coach (2 positions available) encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The 7Th Grade Volleyball - Head Coach (2 positions available) is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the 7Th Grade Volleyball - Head Coach (2 positions available) position at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.CompanyDescription: Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription: class='postingsList' id='p34209_723'> VARSITY BOYS SOCCER COACH JobID: 34209 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 2/3/2026 Location: Davison_High_School Closing Date: Until Filled District: Davison Community Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide EFFECTIVE DATE: 2025/2026 Season ABOUT US: Davison Community Schools, home to approximately 5,500 students and over 1,300 school employees, is a place where excellence in education is a shared priority. With a balanced calendar that supports work-life balance, our high-performing schools thrive on a culture of collaboration, innovation, and student-centered success. We value and invest in our educators and staff, offering ongoing professional development, a supportive team environment, and a strong commitment to positive school culture. If you're passionate about making a difference and growing in your career, DCS is the place for you. QUALIFICATIONS: The following criteria will be used in selecting the position listed above to determine the best qualified coach: Experience as a high school head varsity soccer coach preferred. Previous high school assistant varsity soccer coaching experience preferred. Must have the organizational ability to coordinate a soccer program in grades five through twelve. Must have the ability to work with and give direction to assistant coaches and other related personnel. Consideration will be given to soccer experience at the college level. Must be able to relate well to high school students, parents, high school personnel, and the general public. Previous high school varsity soccer participation preferred. Must have a minimum of two years of soccer coaching experience on the high school level. Must possess a good understanding of soccer coaching situations and strategy as determined by the interviewing process. Demonstrate mental/physical ability and stamina for meeting the requirements of the position. The employee shall remain free of any alcohol or non-prescribed controlled substance in the workplace throughout his/her employment in the District and is expected to maintain regular, reliable, and punctual attendance. Employees will perform related duties as assigned. MANDATORY TRAINING : MHSAA CAP Training Bloodborne Pathogens CPR/AED Certification Concussion Online Class SALARY : Per DEA Master Agreement DIRECT SUPERVISOR : Athletic Director APPLICATION PROCEDURE : Apply online at www.davisonschools.org . Internal applicants are required to provide a letter of intent and resume to the direct supervisor identified on this posting. The district reserves the right to select the person it believes will be the most successful in this position and will be the greatest benefit to the school(s). The Board of Education does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, (including sexual orientation and transgender identity), disability, age, religion, height, weight, marital or family status, military status, ancestry, genetic information or any other legally protected category, (collectively, "Protected Classes"), in its programs and activities, including employment opportunities. The person designated to handle any questions and/or complaints concerning the non-discrimination policy of Davison Community Schools is Director of Human Resources and Operations Christine Kuzinski, 1490 N Oak Rd, Davison, MI 48423 -- (810-591-0808). Job.JobDescription:
The JH Football Coach at Dryden Jr/Sr High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the JH Football Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Dryden Jr/Sr High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The JH Football Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The JH Football Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Dryden Jr/Sr High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Dryden Jr/Sr High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the JH Football Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the JH Football Coach role at Dryden Jr/Sr High School.

Job.JobDescription:
The Swim Coach / Lifeguard (multiple positions available) role at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Swim Coach / Lifeguard (multiple positions available) is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Swim Coach / Lifeguard (multiple positions available) sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools, the Swim Coach / Lifeguard (multiple positions available) is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Swim Coach / Lifeguard (multiple positions available) at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Job.JobDescription:
The Middle School Track Coach at Almont Middle School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Middle School Track Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Almont Middle School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Middle School Track Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Middle School Track Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Middle School Track Coach at Almont Middle School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobDescription:
The Varsity Gils Golf Coach at Almont High School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Varsity Gils Golf Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Almont High School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Varsity Gils Golf Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Varsity Gils Golf Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Varsity Gils Golf Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Almont High School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobDescription: ='postingsList' id='p33824_718'> MIDDLE SCHOOL VOLLEYBALL COACH JobID: 33824 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 10/29/2025 Location: Beecher High School Closing Date: UNTIL FILLED District: Beecher Community School District Additional Information: Show/Hide Only those applicants who meet the qualification and requirements should apply for this position. QUALIFICATIONS: A minimum of two (2) years coaching experience at the middle school or high school level. Strong knowledge and understanding of Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) rules and regulations. Ability to motivate and connect with students from diverse backgrounds. Strong communication skills interacting with students, parents, and staff. Basic knowledge of athletic safety practices and training. Certification in First Aid and CPR (or willingness to obtain). DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Design and implement practice and game plans that build athletic skills, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Mentor student-athletes, focusing on skill improvement, academic success, and personal character. Lead the team in games, make strategic decisions and ensure all safety and game regulations are followed. Priortize student-athlete safety during practices, games, and travel. Strengthen relationships with parents, staff, and the Beecher community to foster team support and engagement. Maintain records, manage equipment, and attend district meetings as required. Communicate with the athletic director to ensure students are maintaining academic eligibility. Assist varsity coaches with fundraising events. The Head Middle School coach and staff will be asked to asist the varsity coaching staff when needed. These duties include but are not limited to: game day help, bookkeeping, game management, etc. REQUIREMENTS: Newly hired employees MUST satisfactorily pass: 1. Police clearance including digital fingerprinting. 2. Reference Checks ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT: In order to complete an application for an athletic coach position, you must be registered with ESS Midwest (formerly PCMI), who is our athletic coach contracted service company. Please direct all questions and inquiries to the Director of Athletics, Eric Cooper, Jr., at 810-280-2025. Job.JobDescription:
The MS Competitive Cheer Head Coach at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the MS Competitive Cheer Head Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The MS Competitive Cheer Head Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the MS Competitive Cheer Head Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The MS Competitive Cheer Head Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.
Job.CompanyDescription: Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
The JV Head Basketball Coach position at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the JV Head Basketball Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The JV Head Basketball Coach at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The JV Head Basketball Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The JV Head Basketball Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the JV Head Basketball Coach position at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.CompanyDescription: Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
The 8Th Grade Boys Basketball Coach at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the 8Th Grade Boys Basketball Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The 8Th Grade Boys Basketball Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The 8Th Grade Boys Basketball Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the 8Th Grade Boys Basketball Coach at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.
Job.CompanyDescription: Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
The Assistant Competitive Cheer Coach role at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Assistant Competitive Cheer Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Assistant Competitive Cheer Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Assistant Competitive Cheer Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Assistant Competitive Cheer Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Assistant Competitive Cheer Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools.

Job.JobDescription:
The Assistant Coach Boys Bowling position at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Assistant Coach Boys Bowling prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools, the Assistant Coach Boys Bowling works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Assistant Coach Boys Bowling encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Assistant Coach Boys Bowling at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
