Job.JobDescription:
The Head Coach 7th Grade Baseball position at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Head Coach 7th Grade Baseball is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Head Coach 7th Grade Baseball encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Head Coach 7th Grade Baseball also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Head Coach 7th Grade Baseball role at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Practices led by the Head Coach 7th Grade Softball are structured and purposeful. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Head Coach 7th Grade Softball recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Head Coach 7th Grade Softball collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Head Coach 7th Grade Softball maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobDescription:
The Assistant Coach - Varsity Girls Soccer at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Assistant Coach - Varsity Girls Soccer plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Assistant Coach - Varsity Girls Soccer expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Assistant Coach - Varsity Girls Soccer supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Assistant Coach - Varsity Girls Soccer may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobTitle: Instructional Consultant for General Education Students K-12
Job.JobDescription: '> Instructional Consultant for General Education Students K-12 JobID: 34107 Position Type: Instructional/ K - 12 Date Posted: 1/9/2026 Location: Special Education Services District: Genesee Intermediate School District Additional Information: Show/Hide Instructional Consultant for General Education Students K-12, SES ABOUT US AND OUR CULTURE At the Genesee Intermediate School District, we are collaborators, leaders, educators, and support staff delivering instructional programs and services in general education, special education, and career technical education to 64,000 students, their families, and over 8,500 educators. GISD values its staff and cultivates positivity in the workplace as we all work together to carry out our mission of leadership, service, and innovation – partnering for success! GISD promotes a collaborative work environment with a strong emphasis on teamwork and professional growth. We are committed to fostering a positive and supportive culture where all staff members feel valued and respected. Our dedication to professional development is demonstrated through initiatives such as resources for professional learning, mentorship programs, and leadership skill development. We are devoted to creating a thriving organizational culture that benefits not only our staff, but also the students and families we serve. To learn more about the GISD culture, please click the link below: GISD Standards, Personalized Service and Quality Programs SALARY AND BENEFITS Salary: $51,373 - $90,934, commensurate with experience and qualifications Benefits: Our comprehensive benefits package includes health insurance with five different plans, dental and vision coverage, life and disability insurance, voluntary insurance options, and a supplemental life program. Employees are entitled to sick time, vacation time, bereavement days, holidays, and longevity pay. Flexible spending accounts (FSA) or healthcare savings accounts (HSA) are available based on the elected medical plan. Additionally, retirement savings plans are provided through the State of Michigan Office of Retirement Services (ORS). These benefits are available after just one full day of employment. LOCATION 5075 Pilgrim Rd. Flint, MI 48507 ROLE AND ESSENTIAL DUTIES The Instructional Consultant provides direct intervention services to students and also serves in a consultative/supportive role with staff, students, parents, and representatives from local districts and community agencies. Responsible for coordination, development, and implementation of instructional practices within assigned areas within a Multi-Tiered System of Supports (MTSS) framework. Essential Duties (May include, but not limited to): Provides direct intervention services for students identified as needing additional tiered support. Provides consultation to classroom staff in areas such as instructional methods, evaluation, and problem solving. Communicates and works cooperatively with school district personnel to promote and facilitate the development and delivery of instruction and behavioral support within a multi-tiered system of support. Participates in district MTSS meetings when appropriate. Assists teachers with designing instruction based on assessment data. Assists in the development, maintenance, and documentation of student’s Plan of Care and related service provision. Prepares reports to facilitate and document student services. Provides teachers resources and training related to instruction and curriculum. Demonstrates knowledge of current research-based methodologies and instructional strategies that engage students. Provides instructional strategies and evidence-based practices using data to monitor progress. Provides coaching and modeling of evidence-based practices to staff. Demonstrates knowledge of using a variety of assessment tools. Provides support in analyzing student assessment data. Consistently models Win-Win leadership. Other Duties: May serve as a resource for the develop

Job.JobDescription: p> ATHLETIC SECRETARY JobID: 34037 Position Type: Secretarial/Clerical/ Program Secretary Date Posted: 12/17/2025 Location: Hamady High School District: Westwood Heights Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Hamady High School — Athletic Secretary Department: Athletics Reports to: Athletic Director Work Year: 10-Month Position Bargaining Unit: WHESPA (Westwood Heights Educational Support Personnel Association) Status: Non-exempt, Classified/Secretarial Position Overview The Athletic Secretary provides comprehensive administrative and logistical support to the Hamady High School Athletic Department, ensuring efficient daily operations, accurate record keeping, regulatory compliance, and timely communication among coaches, student-athletes, families, officials, and visiting schools. Duties & Responsibilities Office & Communication Serve as the primary front-office contact for athletics—greet visitors, answer calls, and respond to inquiries professionally. Prepare and distribute correspondence, memos, schedules, announcements, and meeting materials. Maintain agendas and minutes for athletic meetings and communication with internal and external stakeholders. Scheduling & Event Logistics Maintain the master athletics calendar and publish weekly schedules. Communicate changes, postponements, and cancellations to administration, coaches, families, officials, custodians, transportation, and media partners. Arrange game officials, event workers (ticket takers, clock operators, scorekeepers), and supervision for home events. Coordinate set-up for home contests, banquets, senior recognition ceremonies, award events, and college signing days. Prepare, distribute, and track Seasonal Coach Contracts , ensuring proper documentation and compliance. Transportation & Facilities Coordinate transportation for all athletic events and field trips; manage schedule changes and maintain cost tracking. Oversee and schedule facility use for gyms, fields, and after-school athletics; communicate updates to custodial and maintenance teams. Compliance & Student-Athlete Records Maintain confidential student-athlete files, including physicals, consent forms, emergency contact information, and medical records. Track academic and athletic eligibility under MHSAA and district guidelines. Support coaches in meeting MHSAA and district requirements for participation and compliance. Financial & Ticketing Responsibilities Process purchase orders, requisitions, invoices, deposits, and athletic payments. Reconcile gate receipts and concession revenue; maintain accurate financial logs. Organize ticketing for athletic events and ensure proper event-day and post-event financial procedures. Communication, Media & Display Responsibilities Maintain inventories for equipment, supplies, awards, and office materials. Assist with athletic website updates, community notifications, and announcements as directed. Provide Weekly Updates to the Athletic Image Board, including announcements, highlights, upcoming events, and seasonal recognition items. Additional Responsibilities Support coordination of district-approved communication platforms. Assist coaches with roster creation and essential materials for parent meetings. Contribute to social media updates, sports program materials, and event publications. Perform other duties as assigned in support of the athletic program. Required Qualifications High school diploma or equivalent. Minimum of two (2) years office/secretarial experience, preferably in a school setting. Proficiency in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and athletic scheduling platforms. Strong organizational skills and ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to maintain confidentiality and work professionally with students, staff, parents, and the public. Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of MHSAA rules and athletic eligibility requirements. Experience in bookkeeping or financial re
Job.CompanyDescription: Grand Blanc Commmunity Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription: table class='title' style='padding: 0px; width:99.2%'> Occupational Therapist - Special Education Services - Early On JobID: 34240 Position Type: Special Education/ Occupational Therapist Date Posted: 2/12/2026 Location: Special Education Services District: Genesee Intermediate School District Additional Information: Show/Hide Occupational Therapist - Early On® -SES ABOUT US AND OUR CULTURE At the Genesee Intermediate School District, we are collaborators, leaders, educators, and support staff delivering instructional programs and services in general education, special education, and career technical education to 64,000 students, their families, and over 8,500 educators. GISD values its staff and cultivates positivity in the workplace as we all work together to carry out our mission of leadership, service, and innovation  partnering for success! GISD promotes a collaborative work environment with a strong emphasis on teamwork and professional growth. We are committed to fostering a positive and supportive culture where all staff members feel valued and respected. Our dedication to professional development is demonstrated through initiatives such as resources for professional learning, mentorship programs, and leadership skill development. We are devoted to creating a thriving organizational culture that benefits not only our staff, but also the students and families we serve. Employees are entitled to sick time, vacation time, bereavement days, holidays, and longevity pay. Flexible spending accounts (FSA) or healthcare savings accounts (HSA) are available based on the elected medical plan. Additionally, retirement savings plans are provided through the State of Michigan Office of Retirement Services (ORS). These benefits are available after just one full day of employment. The Occupational Therapist - Early On® a coaching model to share, with parents/caregivers/educational team members, strategies to help the child develop new skills; services are provided in the child's natural environment. Essential Duties (May include, but not limited to): 1. Assists in developing a multi-tiered system of supports to provide developmental and educational experiences for children that impact their development. 2. Develops, implements, and evaluates individualized family service plans for children birth to age three. 3. Provides direct services indicated in the child's individual family service plan. 4. Provides consultation to families and team members to assist in addressing developmental concerns. 5. Identifies the need for and provide cross training for team members. 6. Follows state and federal rules when developing a child's educational plan. 7. Participates in child assessments. 8. Interprets evaluation results for families. 9. Utilizes technology to maintain detailed notes on child's progress. 10. Demonstrates an understanding of sensory processing and coaching caregivers in strategies and activities to improve sensory processing skills. 11. Demonstrates an understanding of feeding difficulties in young children and coaching caregivers in strategies, exercises, and food chaining in order to improve feeding skills. 12. Job.JobDescription: itle' style='padding: 0px; width:99.2%'> Speech and Language Pathologist - Up to $12k 2025/26 Sign On Bonus Available! JobID: 31603 Position Type: Special Education/ Speech and Language Pathologist Date Posted: 2/25/2026 Location: Flint Community Schools Closing Date: Until Filled District: Flint Community Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide The Speech-Language Pathologist plays a crucial role in assessing, diagnosing, and treating communication disorders in students to support their academic and social success. They provide individualized and group therapy, develop intervention plans, and collaborate with teachers, parents, and specialists to integrate language development strategies into the classroom. Additionally, they participate in student evaluations, Individualized Education Program (IEP) meetings, and maintain accurate documentation to ensure compliance with special education requirements. By working closely with families and community resources, they help create a supportive learning environment that fosters student growth and communication skills. Please see attached job description for a detailed list of the essential functions. Salary & Benefits: Teacher Salary Schedule + Up to $12,000 commitment bonus* 183 days per year Health, dental, vision, life and disability insurance. Employees shall be subject to the co-payment of health care costs equal to the difference between the premium cost and the annual cost limitation imposed on public employers. *The commitment bonus shall be distributed to eligible teachers based on available and sustainable funds and payable in accordnce with District policy and guidelines. Education: Master's degree required in speech-language pathology or a valid Michigan teacher's certificate with full approval in the speech and language impaired required. A valid Speech-Language Pathologist license from the State of Michigan. Certificate of Clinical Competence in Speech-Language Pathology (CCC-SLP) from the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA) is required. Clinical fellows with Educational Limited Speech-Language Pathologist license from the State of Michigan will be considered, on-site clinical fellowship supervision is available. Experience: Previous experience with coaching others on strategies to improve language and communications skills in students preferred. An online application is required for all positions. In addition, all applicants must submit the following: Signed cover letter Current resume Copy of transcripts Three signed professional letters of recommendation (written within the last 18 months preferred) These documents must be scanned and uploaded through the online application process. ** Early application is encouraged as positions may close when filled. ** Attachment(s): Speech Language Pathologist.pdf Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/4/2026 Teacher Consultant for Special Education Compliance and Technical Assistance - Special Education Services

Job.JobDescription:
The Esports Assistant coach at Novi High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Esports Assistant coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Novi High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Esports Assistant coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Esports Assistant coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Esports Assistant coach at Novi High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobTitle: Birney K-8 - Appendix B - Girls Basketball Coach (EXTERNAL)
Job.JobDescription: 1'> Birney K-8 - Appendix B - Girls Basketball Coach (EXTERNAL) JobID: 13764 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Activity Sponsor Date Posted: 9/5/2025 Location: District Wide Athletics District: Southfield Public Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Position Title: Girls Middle School Basketball Coach Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Salary: $1,600.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA Start Date/End Date: September 2, 2025 – November 1, 2025 Position Summary: The Middle School Girls’ Basketball Coach is responsible for teaching, guiding, and mentoring students in grades 5–8 in the fundamentals of basketball, promoting teamwork, discipline, and sportsmanship, while ensuring a safe and positive athletic experience. The coach will plan and lead practices, develop game strategies, and foster the personal and athletic development of student-athletes. Key Responsibilities: Plan, organize, and supervise all practices, games, and team events. Teach the fundamentals of basketball, including rules, techniques, and strategies, appropriate to the developmental level of students in grades 5–8. Consistently model and promote good sportsmanship, teamwork, and mutual respect. Treat every player with dignity, fostering a strong sense of self-worth and belonging, while upholding respect for opponents, officials, and spectators always. Always ensure the safety and well-being of all players, including proper conditioning, warm-up, and supervision. Develop and communicate clear expectations for student-athletes and maintain discipline according to school and athletic department policies. Work collaboratively with the Middle School Athletic Director to manage team schedules, equipment, facilities, and transportation or any other needs. Maintain open and timely communication with parents, athletes, and school staff regarding team matters. Ensure all participants meet eligibility, academic, and attendance requirements. Model professional behavior, integrity, and ethical coaching standards always. Support player development by ensuring every athlete has an opportunity to play in every game Comply with all school district, league, and athletic association rules and regulations. Qualifications & Requirements: Knowledge of basketball rules, strategies, and techniques. Ability to effectively teach and motivate young athletes at varying skill levels. Teaching the fundamentals of dribbling, passing, rebounding and the concepts of defense. Ability to prepare for game preparation with set plays, structured offense/defense "Ability to effectively demonstrate proper skills and drills to athletes. Strong organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to work collaboratively with school staff, parents, and community. Demonstrates the ability to inspire confidence in student-athletes by fostering positive emotions such as enthusiasm, joy, and excitement, which contribute to enhanced performance and overall team morale. Must pass all required background checks Prior coaching experience, particularly with youth or middle school athletes. This role focuses on teaching fundamental skills, promoting physical conditioning, encouraging team unity, and instilling sportsmanship. The coach will develop training plans that address multiple events (sprints, and distance), while creating a positive and inclusive environment that supports the athletic and personal development of middle school students. Responsibilities: Design age-appropriate training sessions for various track & field events. Emphasize injury prevention, proper technique, and physical conditioning. Adjust training to accommodate different skill levels and abilities. Teach and reinforce proper mechanics for sprinting, and distance running. Consistently model and promote good sportsmanship, teamwork, and mutual respect. Treat every player with dignity, fostering a strong sense of self-worth and belonging, while upholding respect for opponents, officials, and spectators always. Organize team entries on the slotted date for competitions and ensure athletes are prepared for events. Supervise athletes during travel and competitions, ensuring safety and appropriate behavior. Uphold academic eligibility standards and support the academic success of all team members. Support player development by ensuring every athlete has an opportunity to play in every game. Attend all required district or school coaching meetings and training courses. Model professional behavior, integrity, and ethical coaching standards always. Communicate clearly with parents and school officials regarding schedules, expectations, and updates. Qualifications: Previous coaching or competitive experience in track & field for middle school or club experience. Demonstrate knowledge of sprinting and distance running techniques, including proper lane assignments and effective relay hand-off strategies. Ability to demonstrate leadership, organization, and communication skills. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills to athletes. Demonstrates the ability to inspire/motivate confidence in student-athletes by fostering positive emotions such as enthusiasm, joy, and excitement, which contribute to enhanced performance and overall team morale. Background check clearance required. Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/4/2026 Levey - Appendix B - Middle School Track Coach (EXTERNAL)

yle='padding: 0px; width:99.2%'> Thompson K-8 - Appendix B - Middle School Sideline/Competitive Cheer Coach (EXTERNAL) JobID: 13770 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Activity Sponsor Date Posted: 9/5/2025 Location: District Wide Athletics District: Southfield Public Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Position Title: Middle School Sideline/Competitive Cheer Coach Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Salary: $700.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA Start Date/End Date: December 3, 2025 – February 14, 2026 Job Summary: The Cheer Coach is responsible for organizing, planning, and leading the middle school cheerleading program. This includes teaching cheers, stunts, and dance routines; promoting school spirit; always ensuring safety; and fostering teamwork, discipline, and leadership among team members. The coach will work closely with the athletic department, school administration, and parents to support athletic events, competitions, and school functions. Key Responsibilities: Plan and conduct practices focused on cheer techniques, stunting, tumbling, and performance. Lead the cheer team in promoting school spirit at games, pep rallies, special events and focus on crowd engagement. Choreograph routines for halftime shows, assemblies, and competitions. Strong knowledge of safety standards and regulations for cheering and tumbling rules. Always emphasize safety, particularly during stunts and tumbling activities. Consistently model and promote good sportsmanship, teamwork, and mutual respect. Treat every player with dignity, fostering a strong sense of self-worth and belonging, while upholding respect for opponents, officials, and spectators always. Promote positive team culture, including sportsmanship, building trust, responsibility, and leadership. Ensure all participants meet eligibility, academic, and attendance requirements. Support player development by ensuring every athlete has an opportunity to cheer in every game. Manage uniforms and equipment. Maintain open and professional communication with Middle School AD, student-athletes, parents, and school staff. Coaches are responsible for conducting parental meetings. Supervise athletes during all practices, games, competitions, and travel. Model professional behavior, integrity, and ethical coaching standards always. Support and enforce all school, district, and league policies. Qualifications: Prior experience as a cheer coach at the elementary or club level required. Creative and energic Safety-conscious and detail-oriented Knowledge of cheer techniques, stunts, and performance skills. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills to athletes. Must pass all required background checks Must demonstrate strong leadership, organizational, supervisory, and communication abilities. Demonstrates the ability to inspire/motivate confidence in student-athletes by fostering positive emotions such as enthusiasm, joy, and excitement, which contribute to enhanced performance and overall team morale. Key Responsibilities: Plan, organize, and supervise all practices, games, and team events. Teach the fundamentals of basketball, including rules, techniques, and strategies, appropriate to the developmental level of students in grades 5–8. "Consistently model and promote good sportsmanship, teamwork, and mutual respect. Treat every player with dignity, fostering a strong sense of self-worth and belonging, while upholding respect for opponents, officials, and spectators always. Always ensure the safety and well-being of all players, including proper conditioning, warm-up, and supervision. Develop and communicate clear expectations for student-athletes and maintain discipline according to school and athletic department policies. Work collaboratively with the Middle School Athletic Director to manage team schedules, equipment, facilities, and transportation or any other needs. Maintain open and timely communication with parents, athletes, and school staff regarding team matters. Model professional behavior, integrity, and ethical coaching standards always. Uphold academic eligibility standards and support the academic success of all team members. Support player development by ensuring every athlete has an opportunity to play in every game. Attend all required district or school coaching meetings and training courses. Comply with all school district, league, and athletic association rules and regulations. Qualifications: Knowledge of basketball rules, strategies, and techniques. Ability to effectively teach and motivate young athletes at varying skill levels. "Ability to effectively demonstrate proper skills and drills to athletes. Strong Leadership, organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to work collaboratively with school staff, parents, and community. Demonstrates the ability to inspire confidence in student-athletes by fostering positive emotions such as enthusiasm, joy, and excitement, which contribute to enhanced performance and overall team morale. Background check clearance required. Prior coaching experience, particularly with youth or middle school athletes. tle' style='padding: 0px; width:99.2%'> Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Assistant Basketball Coach/Girls Sub-Varsity JobID: 14436 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Activity Sponsor Date Posted: 11/16/2025 Location: District Wide Athletics District: Southfield Public Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Job Title: Girls Sub-Varsity/Assistant Basketball Coach Location: Southfield A&T High School Reports To: Athletic Director Start Date/End Dates: November 2025 – March 2026 Salary: $2,700.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season) Job Overview: The Girls’ Basketball Coach is responsible for overseeing and managing all aspects of the girls’ basketball program, including coaching, player development, team coordination, and communication with players, parents, and staff. The ideal candidate will be dedicated to building a competitive and supportive team environment, fostering the personal and athletic growth of student-athletes, and promoting the values of teamwork, sportsmanship, and discipline. Responsibilities: Coaching: Lead and assist the Head Coach in the girls’ basketball team practices and during games, providing skill development, strategy, and motivation to players. Player Development: Foster the growth of individual players in both athletic and personal aspects, promoting physical conditioning, mental toughness, and sportsmanship. Team Management: Develop and implement training sessions, drills, and practice plans that improve player performance and team cohesion. Game Day Management: Prepare the team for competitions, make real-time decisions during games, and conduct post-game analysis and feedback sessions. Scheduling & Coordination: Organize and manage the team’s practice schedule, game schedule, travel arrangements, and other logistical details. Communication: Maintain open communication with players, parents, athletic department staff, and other school officials to provide updates and foster team morale. Compliance & Safety: Ensure that the team follows all safety protocols, academic eligibility requirements, and sportsmanship expectations set by the school, district, and governing athletic organizations. Growing the Program: Work closely with the Athletic Director and the Middle Schools for retention and preparation for the next season. Fundraising & Team Support: Assist in fundraising efforts for the program, including organizing events, seeking sponsorships, and managing team budgets. Mentorship: Serve as a role model and mentor for student-athletes, encouraging academic achievement, discipline, and respect both on and off the court. Qualifications: Previous experience coaching basketball at the high school or college level preferred. Strong understanding of basketball techniques, strategies, and game rules. Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to motivate and inspire student-athletes to reach their full potential. Strong commitment to the development of student-athletes both on and off the court. Ability to work effectively with a diverse group of students, parents, and staff. Must pass a background check and meet all district and state coaching requirements. Physical Requirements: Ability to participate in and demonstrate basketball techniques during practice and games. Ability to lift and carry coaching equipment as needed. Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/4/2026 Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Assistant Basketball Coach/Boys Sub-Varsity

Job.JobDescription: 9_46171'> Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Baseball Head Coach JobID: 14439 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Activity Sponsor Date Posted: 11/16/2025 Location: District Wide Athletics District: Southfield Public Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Job Title: Baseball Head Coach Reports To: Athletic Director Location: Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: November 2025- March 2026 Salary: $4,200 Stipend per SEA/CBA paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar. Job Summary: The Head Baseball Coach is responsible for developing, leading, and maintaining a competitive high school baseball program that aligns with the mission and goals of the school’s athletic department. The coach will provide leadership that promotes sportsmanship, teamwork, skill development, and academic success among student-athletes. Key Responsibilities: Organize, plan, and oversee all aspects of the baseball program, including practices, games, and offseason conditioning. Teach proper baseball fundamentals, mechanics, and strategies appropriate to high school competition. Develop detailed practice plans that focus on skill progression, team cohesion, and player safety. Foster a culture of academic accountability, teamwork, sportsmanship, and respect. Monitor and support the academic progress of student-athletes to ensure eligibility and success in the classroom. Supervise all team activities, including travel to away games and tournaments. Manage and maintain baseball equipment, uniforms, and facilities. Communicate effectively with players, parents, and school officials regarding schedules, expectations, and program needs. Ensure adherence to district, league, and state association rules and regulations. Work collaboratively with assistant coaches to ensure consistent instruction and program development. Serve as a positive representative of the baseball program within the school and local community. Qualifications: Previous baseball coaching experience at the high school/club level or higher required. Comprehensive knowledge of baseball fundamentals, techniques, and strategies. CPR, First Aid, and Concussion Awareness certification (or willingness to obtain before the season). Strong organizational, communication, and leadership skills. Demonstrated ability to motivate and perform skills and drills. Must meet all district and state athletic association certification and training requirement Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/4/2026 Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Baseball Assistant Coach

Monitor academic progress and eligibility of all players; encourage balance between athletics and academics. Ensure compliance with district, league, and state athletic association regulations. Maintain inventory of uniforms, equipment, and supplies and ensure safety standards are met. Supervise athletes during all practices, games, travel, and team events Qualifications: Previous coaching and/or competitive softball experience required at the high school level/club or higher required. Strong knowledge of softball rules, techniques, and game strategy. Valid CPR, First Aid, and Concussion Awareness certification (or ability to obtain prior to season). Excellent communication, leadership, and organizational skills. Demonstrated ability to motivate and teach athletes skills and drills. Must meet all district, league, and state athletic association certification requirements. Job.JobTitle: Boys Lacrosse JV Head Coach - NHS
Job.JobDescription:
The Boys Lacrosse JV Head Coach - NHS role at Farmington Public Schools is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Boys Lacrosse JV Head Coach - NHS is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Farmington Public Schools are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Boys Lacrosse JV Head Coach - NHS listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Boys Lacrosse JV Head Coach - NHS encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Boys Lacrosse JV Head Coach - NHS follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Farmington Public Schools, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Boys Lacrosse JV Head Coach - NHS at Farmington Public Schools, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

The Football - Freshman Head Coach at Farmington Public Schools is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Football - Freshman Head Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Farmington Public Schools use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Football - Freshman Head Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Football - Freshman Head Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Football - Freshman Head Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Farmington Public Schools. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

The Varsity Baseball Head Coach position at Oak Park High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Varsity Baseball Head Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Varsity Baseball Head Coach at Oak Park High School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Oak Park High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Varsity Baseball Head Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Varsity Baseball Head Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Varsity Baseball Head Coach position at Oak Park High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

The Varsity Softball Head Coach position at Oak Park High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Varsity Softball Head Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Oak Park High School, the Varsity Softball Head Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Varsity Softball Head Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Varsity Softball Head Coach at Oak Park High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

The 5th Grade Basketball Coach at Pepper Elementary is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the 5th Grade Basketball Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Pepper Elementary reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The 5th Grade Basketball Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the 5th Grade Basketball Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the 5th Grade Basketball Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Pepper Elementary. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Varsity Boys Head Golf Coach - Kettering and Mott High Schools is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Varsity Boys Head Golf Coach - Kettering and Mott High Schools position at Farmington Public Schools is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

The Varsity Football Coach - Kettering at Farmington Public Schools is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Varsity Football Coach - Kettering are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Farmington Public Schools use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Varsity Football Coach - Kettering recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Varsity Football Coach - Kettering collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Varsity Football Coach - Kettering maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Farmington Public Schools. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: Head Boys Cross Country Coach - Mott
Job.JobDescription:
The Head Boys Cross Country Coach - Mott at Farmington Public Schools is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Head Boys Cross Country Coach - Mott manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Farmington Public Schools, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. Job.JobDescription: id='p14760_46152'> JV Softball Head Coach - Berkley High School JobID: 14760 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 12/2/2025 Location: Berkley High School District: Berkley School District Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION OPEN: JV Softball Head Coach LOCATION: Berkley High School 2026 Spring Season COMPENSATION: Commensurate with experience per Berkley Athletics Pay Schedule QUALIFICATIONS: Previous coaching or playing experience in softball Demonstrated ability to build positive relationships with students Strong organizational and communication skills CPR/First Aid Certified (or willingness to obtain) DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Plan and run daily practices that build skills, confidence, and teamwork Create a positive, inclusive team environment that supports student-athlete development Teach foundational softball skills, game concepts, and strategies appropriate for the JV level Set clear expectations focused on effort, sportsmanship, and accountability Communicate effectively with student-athletes, families, and the varsity coaching staff Lead the JV team in all contests and assist with game-day preparation Supervise athletes before, during, and after practices and games Collaborate with the varsity head coach to ensure program alignment Ensure student-athletes meet all team, school, and MHSAA requirements APPLICATION: Interested candidates email resume and cover letter to Matt Rawlik at matthew.rawlik@berkleyschools.org DEADLINE FOR APPLICATIONS: Until filled District Vision Our inclusive culture will inspire, empower and lead all learners to realize their full potential and make a positive difference in their future and community. Commitment The Berkley School District is committed to keeping diversity, equity and inclusion at the forefront of its goal of creating an environment where all staff, students and community members feel a sense of belonging. Per School Safety Legislation, each individual, who is either employed full-time or part-time, or is assigned to regularly and continuously work under contract, shall be fingerprinted for the purpose of performing a criminal history background check. Prior to employment, selected candidate must complete the fingerprinting process at the applicant's expense. Berkley School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, religion, marital status, height, weight or any disability in its educational programs, activities or employment as required by Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973. The Girls Varsity Swim Coach position at Bloomfield Hills High School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Girls Varsity Swim Coach is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Bloomfield Hills High School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Girls Varsity Swim Coach encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Girls Varsity Swim Coach also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Girls Varsity Swim Coach role at Bloomfield Hills High School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Job.JobTitle: Boys Cross Country – Varsity Head Coach
Job.JobDescription: d='p14663_46155'> Boys Cross Country – Varsity Head Coach JobID: 14663 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 11/17/2025 Location: Brandon High School District: Brandon School District Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION : Boys Cross Country – Varsity Head Coach – req LOCATION : Brandon High School DESCRIPTION OF THE DISTRICT : The Brandon School District is a mid-size school district nestled in Northwest Oakland County. The district has approximately 1,000 students in junior kindergarten-5 th grade between the two elementary schools, approximately 600 at the middle school level and approximately 850 at the high school. This small, tight knit community has a strong connection to its schools and is passionate about the education of its youth. Brandon High School sends 85-90% of its graduates to post-secondary school each year, it was ranked in the 82 nd percentile of all schools in the State on the top-to-bottom rankings and it was awarded as a Silver Medal Winner by the US News. Our elementary schools are both one-to-one technology are each near the 83rd percentile in State rankings. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS : High School Diploma Working with students in a similar capacity is preferred KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Must have documented evidence of organizational skills, knowledge, experience, preparation and aptitudes in the above sport commensurate with the level of the position Must have demonstrated ability to work with and motivate young people and adults Must be available during the time requirements of the position Certification in CPR/AED preferred RESPONSIBILITIES: Directly responsible for all matters relative to the program including but not limited to, player personnel, practice sessions, care and maintenance of equipment and supervision of players during practice, games, travel and other team functions. Be knowledgeable of and consistently enforce school, District, League and state association (MHSAA) rules and policies. Effectively communicate with players, officials, fellow coaches, parents, administrators and community members. Ensure that the safety and welfare of the athletes is a top priority in the organization and implementation of practice and game situations. Be responsive to instructions and directions of the district Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Present a professional image and provide a positive role model in terms of personal habits, language and conduct. Responsible for press releases and public relations. Other duties as assigned. REPORTS TO : Athletic Director WORKDAY/WEEK : 20-25 hours per week in season, 10-12 hours per week out of season STARTING DATE : June 1, 2026 COMPENSATION : Per the BEA Master Agreement, Athletics Schedule C.2 POSTING DATE: November 17, 2025 POSTING DEADLINE : Internal: Until Filled External: Until Filled The Brandon Board of Education is committed to a policy of non-discrimination in relation to race, color, sex, age, religion, height, weight, gender identity, marital status, disability and national origin. For all concerns and complaints for issues relating to Title IX, Section 504, the Age Discrimination Act and Title II, please contact Megan Koslowski, Title IX Coordinator at 1025 S. Ortonville Road, Ortonville, MI 48462, (248) 627-1800. Email To A Friend Print Version Openings as of 3/4/2026 Varsity Hockey Coach

The Middle School Track Coach role at Clawson Middle School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Middle School Track Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Clawson Middle School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Middle School Track Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Middle School Track Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Middle School Track Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Clawson Middle School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Middle School Track Coach at Clawson Middle School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

The 25-ATH-37 Middle School Assistant Track Coach role at Farmington STEAM Academy emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the 25-ATH-37 Middle School Assistant Track Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Farmington STEAM Academy, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The 25-ATH-37 Middle School Assistant Track Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Farmington STEAM Academy at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The 25-ATH-37 Middle School Assistant Track Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The 25-ATH-37 Middle School Assistant Track Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The 25-ATH-37 Middle School Assistant Track Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Farmington STEAM Academy.

ingsList' id='p15008_46162'> 2025-26 Junior High Baseball Coach JobID: 15008 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 1/8/2026 Location: Hazel Park Junior High (High-Needs School) District: Hazel Park Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide 2025/2026 Spring Season Junior High Baseball Coach Position Overview: We are seeking dedicated and enthusiastic individuals to join our team as a High School Athletic Coach. You will be responsible for coaching and mentoring scholar-athletes in various sports, fostering skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a positive impact on the lives of young athletes while contributing to the success of our high school sports program. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. You will be responsible for coaching and mentoring scholar-athletes in various sports, fostering skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a positive impact on the lives of young athletes while contributing to the success of our high school sports program. Responsibilities: Coach Scholar-Athletes: Provide instruction, guidance, and mentorship to student-athletes in designated sports, including but not limited to football, basketball, soccer, volleyball, baseball, softball, track and field, wrestling, swimming, tennis, and golf. Plan and Execute Practices: Develop and implement comprehensive practice plans focused on skill development, strategy, and conditioning. Create a positive and engaging practice environment that fosters learning and growth. Strategic Game Planning: Develop game strategies, tactics, and playbooks to maximize team performance during competitions. Make real-time adjustments and decisions during games to optimize team success. Player Development: Assess individual player strengths and weaknesses and provide personalized coaching to enhance skill development and performance. Encourage continuous improvement and set realistic goals for player development. Team Building and Leadership: Foster a positive team culture based on sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork. Promote leadership skills, communication, and collaboration among team members. Academic Emphasis: Instill the importance of academic excellence and balance between athletics and academics among scholar-athletes. Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to re
