Player Development: Assess individual player strengths and weaknesses and provide personalized coaching to enhance skill development and performance. Encourage continuous improvement and set realistic goals for player development. Team Building and Leadership: Foster a positive team culture based on sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork. Promote leadership skills, communication, and collaboration among team members. Academic Emphasis: Instill the importance of academic excellence and balance between athletics and academics among scholar-athletes. Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Job.JobTitle: Head Boys Track and Field Coach - Muir Middle School
Job.JobDescription: 91_46164'> Head Boys Track and Field Coach - Muir Middle School JobID: 15291 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 2/5/2026 Location: Muir Middle School District: Huron Valley Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION DESCRIPTION Huron Valley Schools is seeking an enthusiastic, energetic, and innovative Head Boys' Track & Field Coach. Position Type: Athletics/Activities/Coaching Location : Muir Middle School Salary: Per contract Schedule B Reports To : Athletic Director Status : School Year 2025 - 2026 Start Date: ASAP MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS Able to instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training necessary for athletes to realize a degree of individual and team success Coaching experience preferred Playing experience at the high school or college level is preferred ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites Perform duties which may be assigned by the Athletic Director Instills in each player a respect for equipment, and school property The position includes other unusual aspects such as extended time, risk injury factors and due process predicaments Implement and design clinics in order to promote the sport Schedule all practices, clinics, camps and competitions Continue to grow professionally by attending seminars and clinics Maintains discipline and works to increase morale and cooperation EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE: High School diploma and/or related experience. LANGUAGE, MATHEMATICAL AND REASONING SKILLS: Ability to speak and write effectively. Ability to apply mathematical concepts to practical situations. Ability to carry out instructions furnished in written or oral format and deal with problems involving several concrete variables in standardized situations. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit, talk, or hear. The employee is occasionally required to stand, walk and reach with hands or arms. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision and ability to adjust and focus. WORK ENVIRONMENT: The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. METHOD OF APPLICATION A cover letter is required in order to be considered for this position and should be attached as the first page of your resume. The cover letter should outline skills and experience that directly relate to the qualifications for the position. Please send letter of intent and coaching resume to: Jim Marszalek Milford High School Athletic Director jim.marszalek@hvs.org Inquiries should be addressed to: Jeanna Klebba Human Resources Administrative Assistant 2390 South Milford Rd., Highland, MI 48357 248.684.8217 (TELEPHONE) • 248.684.8266 (FAX) E-MAIL: jeanna.klebba@hvs.org Jeanette Wenger, Chief Human Resource Officer and TitleVI Officer In compliance with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, Title II, the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, and the Americans with Disability Act of 1990, it is the policy of the Huron Valley School district that no person shall, on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin or ancestry, sex, age, disability, height, weight, or marital status be excluded from participation in, be denied benefits of, or be subjected to discrimination during any program or activity or in employment. The Girls Varsity Golf Coach at Royal Oak High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Girls Varsity Golf Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Royal Oak High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Girls Varsity Golf Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Girls Varsity Golf Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Royal Oak High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Royal Oak High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Girls Varsity Golf Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Girls Varsity Golf Coach role at Royal Oak High School.

The Girls Assistant Varsity Lacrosse Coach role at Royal Oak High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Girls Assistant Varsity Lacrosse Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Royal Oak High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Girls Assistant Varsity Lacrosse Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Girls Assistant Varsity Lacrosse Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Girls Assistant Varsity Lacrosse Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Royal Oak High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Girls Assistant Varsity Lacrosse Coach at Royal Oak High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

The Softball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School at Royal Oak Middle School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Softball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Royal Oak Middle School, the Softball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Softball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Royal Oak Middle School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Softball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

The Baseball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School position at Royal Oak Middle School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Baseball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Royal Oak Middle School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Baseball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Baseball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Baseball Coach-Royal Oak Middle School role at Royal Oak Middle School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Royal Oak Middle School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The 8th Grade Girls Volleyball Coach encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The 8th Grade Girls Volleyball Coach also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the 8th Grade Girls Volleyball Coach role at Royal Oak Middle School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

The JV Assistant Football Coach role at Royal Oak High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the JV Assistant Football Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Royal Oak High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The JV Assistant Football Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the JV Assistant Football Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The JV Assistant Football Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Royal Oak High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the JV Assistant Football Coach at Royal Oak High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

The Girls Varsity Volleyball Coach at Royal Oak High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Girls Varsity Volleyball Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Royal Oak High School, the Girls Varsity Volleyball Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Girls Varsity Volleyball Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Royal Oak High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Girls Varsity Volleyball Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Job.JobDescription: class='postingsList' id='p15465_46208'> Varsity Baseball Coach JobID: 15465 Position Type: Athletics/Activities/ Coaching Date Posted: 2/25/2026 Location: Madison High School District: Madison District Public Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide INTERNAL/EXTERNAL POSTING JOB TITLE: Varsity Baseball Coach LOCATION: Madison High School START DATE: March 2026 SALARY: Stipend based on MEA Collective Bargaining Agreement DESCRIPTION Madison District Public Schools is seeking a passionate and dedicated individual to serve as the Varsity Baseball Coach at Madison High School. The successful candidate will provide leadership in developing a competitive baseball program that emphasizes sportsmanship, teamwork, academic success, and character development while supporting the overall mission of the district's athletic program. QUALIFICATIONS Previous baseball coaching experience preferred Strong understanding of baseball fundamentals and team development Ability to build positive relationships with students, parents, and staff Commitment to promoting a positive and inclusive team environment Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills CPR/First Aid certification KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Organize, supervise, and coach the high school varsity baseball team. Develop practice plans focused on skill development, conditioning, and team strategy. Promote positive sportsmanship and student-athlete character development. Ensure compliance with all district policies, athletic department procedures, and MHSAA rules and regulations. Supervise student-athletes during practices, games, and team-related activities. Collaborate with sub-varsity coaches to support program continuity and athlete development. Maintain appropriate communication with athletes, parents, staff, and administration. Monitor student-athlete academic progress and promote academic achievement. Coordinate equipment inventory, uniforms, and program needs. Attend required meetings, trainings, and professional development as assigned. Ensure the safety and well-being of all participants. METHOD OF APPLICATION Complete application process with Oakland Human Resources Consortium (OHRC) Upload letter of interest, current resume, and letters of recommendation Internal candidates interested in applying for an open position must submit a letter of interest to Pam Vermiglio, Assistant Superintendent, at pam.vermiglio@madisondistrict.org . Job.JobTitle: Schedule C - JV Asst. Pom Coach
Job.JobDescription:
The Schedule C - JV Asst. Pom Coach at South Lyon High School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Schedule C - JV Asst. Pom Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at South Lyon High School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Schedule C - JV Asst. Pom Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Schedule C - JV Asst. Pom Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Schedule C - JV Asst. Pom Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at South Lyon High School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Master's degree or higher in the field of administration, with course work in educational leadership and/or curriculum instruction. Valid State of Michigan teaching certificate. Secondary building administrative/leadership experience, preferred. Minimum of four years of successful teaching experience. Demonstrated knowledge of current educational issues and best practices. Demonstrated leadership and success in parent/community relations, staff development, and human relations. Demonstrated ability to effectively communicate (written and oral) with stakeholders. Knowledge of current best practice instructional strategies & ability to improve teacher instruction. Knowledge of International Baccalaureate, Career Tech Ed, and Early College Programs. Demonstrated knowledge of current federal and state laws and policies. Possess an understanding of high school educational philosophy. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Board may find appropriate and acceptable. Essential Duties & Responsibilities: Work to maintain an effective learning climate in the school. Work to establish and maintain a favorable relationship with local community groups and individuals to foster understanding and solicit support for overall school objectives and programs, and to interpret and implement Board policies and administrative directives. Help to strengthen assessment policy, practice and procedures that align with IB philosophy, standards and best practice. Help to implement common assessment protocol use of data within PLCs to improve teaching and learning. Identify appropriate professional development and allocation of resources to support best practices for various programs and teaching framework: vertical teaming of staff (6-12); horizontal planning and collaboration; continued collaboration at both building and district levels. Research program initiatives that support the Portrait of a Graduate. Assist building and district administrator in developing master schedule(s) that support allprograms and curriculum. Support instructional coaches and coordinators to help ensure alignment with instructional practices. Develop IB community and informational resources. Serve as an administrative team member in the administration, supervision, and evaluation of administrative, teaching, ancillary staff, and support staff. Work with instructional staff to initiate, design and implement programs to meet assessed needs of students. Serve as an administrative team leader in meetings with professional and support staff for the effective operation of the school, as well as participation in district meetings, as required. Work with counseling and support staff to assess the mental health needs of the student body. Assist in the development of school-wide SEL lessons and student mental health supports. Assist in the management and supervision of students in the areas of attendance, conduct, safety and security, discipline, etc. Assist in the development and implementation of program po

Behavior Specialist JobID: 14930 Position Type: Certified Student Support Services/ Certifed Other Date Posted: 12/19/2025 Location: Oakwood Elementary District: Brandon School District Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION : Behavior Specialist LOCATION : Oakwood Elementary, District-wide DESCRIPTION OF THE DISTRICT : The Brandon School District is a mid-size school district nestled in Northwest Oakland County. The district has approximately 1,000 students in junior kindergarten-5 th grade between the two elementary schools, approximately 600 at the middle school level and approximately 850 at the high school. This small, tight knit community has a strong connection to its schools and is passionate about the education of its youth. Brandon High School sends 85-90% of its graduates to post-secondary school each year, it was ranked in the 82 nd percentile of all schools in the State on the top-to-bottom rankings and it was awarded as a Silver Medal Winner by the US News. Our elementary schools are both one-to-one technology are each near the 83rd percentile in State rankings. POSITION DESCRIPTION: Brandon School District is seeking a Behavior Specialist responsible for providing guidance and expertise to teachers and support staff on strategies for supporting student social and emotional growth, as well as effective behavior management techniques. The Behavior Specialist will also provide ongoing support to school leadership teams and principals on the critical components and best practices of behavior management within classrooms. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS : Valid Michigan professional certification in an area such as school social worker, school psychologist, etc. Experience in supporting success for students with significant behavioral challenges as related to a variety of conditions, including students with and without disabilities; experience working with students with Autism Spectrum Disorder and/or Emotional Impairment preferred. Excellent organizational and analytical skills, task oriented, detail oriented and deadline oriented. Strong oral and written communication skills. Ability to collect and analyze data. Knowledge of agency supports and services for students with complex behaviors. Strong leadership skills: motivating, delegating, reliability, follow-through. Ability to develop positive rapport with staff, students, and parents. Must be CPI trained or willing to be CPI trained within 30 days of hire and annually update certification. RESPONSIBILITIES : Provide guidance and expertise to teachers and other classroom staff on strategies for supporting student social and emotional growth, as well as effective behavior management techniques. Conduct training sessions and provide ongoing support to school leadership teams and principals on the critical components and best practices of behavior management within classrooms. Assist staff in analyzing student behavior data to identify patterns and trends, and collaborate with them to develop Behavior Intervention Plans (BIPs) tailored to individual student needs. Offer guidance and support to paraprofessionals working with students who may require additional behavioral support. Lead initiatives aimed at promoting positive behavioral supports within the school community, including training sessions and modeling of effective strategies. Assists with leading school-wide Positive Behavioral Interventions and Supports (PBIS), focusing on proactive behavior management, data analysis, staff training, and fostering a positive climate through evidence-based practices. Facilitates PBIS teams, analyzes behavioral data to inform strategies, coaches teachers on classroom management. Implements Tier 1 interventions ensuring fidelity, and building strong school-wide systems to improve student social-emotional competence and academic success. Assists building administrators in assessing systems and guiding teams in data collection, ensuring accurate scoring and helping to translate scor

class='postingsList' id='p15477_46164'> Transition Coordinator JobID: 15477 Position Type: Special Education/ Special Education Consultant Date Posted: 2/26/2026 Location: Huron Valley Schools District Wide District: Huron Valley Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide POSITION DESCRIPTION Huron Valley Schools is seeking a Transition Coordinator who values collaboration and is well-versed in best practices within special education transition services. This individual will support districts, educators, and families at the secondary level (middle school, high school, and adult transition) to achieve meaningful post-school outcomes through regulatory compliance and the utilization of best practices. The selected candidate will demonstrate success in building relationships and in building futures one student at a time. Position Type: Coordinator for Categorical Programming Location: Huron Valley Schools District Salary: Per HVEA Contract Reports To : Executive Director of Student Support Services Work Year: 26/27 Start Date: 08/25/26 Date Posted : 02/26/2026 Closing Date: Until filled MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS Master's degree from an accredited university in education or a field of study related to special education Valid Michigan Teaching Certificate in Autism Spectrum Disorder (SV), Emotional Impairments (SE), Cognitive Impairments (SA) and/or Learning Disabilities (SM). OR fully licensed in a related field (school psychology, speech language pathology, school social work, occupational therapy, etc.) Michigan Department of Education (MDE) Transition Coordinator Full Approval or ability to obtain Minimum of five (5) years of professional experience as a special education teacher, in a vocational rehabilitation role, or in a related field. Minimum of three (3) years experience Completing Transition sections of an IEP Minimum of three (3) years experience training Paraeducators and/or Peers Demonstrated ability to work cooperatively and confidentially with general and special education teachers, administrators, parents and support staff members. Valid Michigan driver's license and the ability to travel to local schools, job sites, and community businesses throughout Oakland County Basic knowledge of CTE, EDPs, Personal Curriculums and CBI Basic understanding of community mental health and vocational rehabilitation systems Demonstrate ability to work with high stress situations and have a strong ability to seek creative solutions. Strong written and verbal communication skills to lend toward solution seeking with staff, parents and advocates on behalf of students and the district. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS Regulatory Compliance: Establish, implement, and maintain policies and procedures to ensure district compliance with Federal, State, and Oakland County regulations related to transition aged students with disabilities. IEP Development: Assist, train, and monitor the completion of the Transition Services Component of the IEP, including the development of measurable postsecondary goals. Agency Collaboration: Serve as the primary staff liaison with Michigan Rehabilitation Services (MRS) and other participating agencies to assist students and families in understanding post-high school options. Instructional Leadership: Provide professional development and coaching to staff regarding transition best practices, including work-based learning and interagency collaboration. Attend Oakland Schools OCTA meetings monthly and report learning back to district administration. Work-Based Learning: Coordinate the development of robust Work-Based Learning (WBL) programs at the high school and post-secondary level, ensuring job site development and student supervision. Data Management: Work with the ISD to collect state performance plan transition data (SPP #13 and #14), use of catamaran, and maintain accurate, confidential student case files. Community Relations: Establish and maintain a robust network of school-community partnerships and serve on appropr

District: Lamphere Schools Additional Information: Show/Hide Lamphere Schools is contracting the services of Edustaff for Long-Term Substitute Teacher Position: Long Term Sub - Elementary Self-Contained ASD Special Education Teacher Location: Simonds Elementary School Pay Rate: $225/day Preferred Qualifications: Certified in Early Childhood Education (ZA), Elementary K-5 All Subjects (K-8 All Subjects Self-Contained Classrooms) (ZG), or Elementary K-8 All Subjects (ZC) An endorsement in Autism Spectrum Disorder (SV) A Bachelor's degree with a major in a specific Special Education area, or have earned credit in coursework equivalent to that required for a major Such alternatives to the above qualifications that the administration may find appropriate and acceptable Position Requirements: Ability to effectively engage young children and to model and teach strategies/interventions for students with ASD Ability to create lessons that target individual student goals as determined by their IEP team Ability to complete student progress reports and report cards Ability to maintain detailed documentation Ability to structure and organize time so as to meet required deadlines Proficiency in Google Suite applications (Docs, Sheets, Slides, Forms, etc.) and online sourcing tools, with functional knowledge of internet use for search platforms Ability to withstand the strain of teaching Must be reliable and punctual, with excellent work-related attendance record Such alternatives to the above requirements that the administration may find appropriate and acceptable Knowledge of child development/milestones and effective ASD strategies and teaching practices Knowledge of local, state, and federal special education laws/guidelines Knowledge of various assessment tools and the ability to administer them and interpret results Ability to create classroom schedules and plans for paraprofessionals Ability to positively and effectively communicate and collaborate with colleagues, students, and their families Position Responsibilities: Plan, prepare, and implement instruction to K-5 students with ASD Collect student data, make instructional decisions,and create lesson plans based on the data Work collaboratively with ancillary and auxiliary staff regarding caseload students Schedule and facilitate IEP meetings Along with the rest of the special education team, plan lessons and implement schedules that use student strengths and interests to achieve and set individualized goals Work with and guide special education and IEP team members to collect data and report student progress on report cards and progress reports. Provide ongoing coaching and consultation to paraprofessionals and other staff to build the capacity to serve students with disabilities in their Least Restrictive Environment Administer all required evaluations for students receiving services, e.g.,district and state assessments; record and submit all data within the mandated timelines Maintain regular and punctual attendance Provide all Supplementary Aids and Services as required by individual student IEPs Follow all Behavior Intervention Plans Consult with ancillary staff to address student needs Schedule and attend Problem Solving Team meetings as necessary Such alternatives to the above responsibilities that the administration may find appropriate and acceptable Qualified Applicant Procedure: Certified Positions click here Non-Certified Positions click here Selected candidates will be required to have a district paid fingerprint criminal history check processed through the Michigan State Police and FBI. The Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) at Holt High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Holt High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) at Holt High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

The ASST. FOOTBALL COACH - PROVINE at Jackson Public Sch. Dis. is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the ASST. FOOTBALL COACH - PROVINE is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Jackson Public Sch. Dis., coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The ASST. FOOTBALL COACH - PROVINE helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The ASST. FOOTBALL COACH - PROVINE works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Jackson Public Sch. Dis. operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Jackson Public Sch. Dis. with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the ASST. FOOTBALL COACH - PROVINE reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the ASST. FOOTBALL COACH - PROVINE role at Jackson Public Sch. Dis..

Job.JobTitle: HEAD SOCCER COACH (BOYS) - PROVINE HIGH SCHOOL
Job.JobDescription:
The HEAD SOCCER COACH (BOYS) - PROVINE HIGH SCHOOL position at Jackson Public Sch. Dis. is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the HEAD SOCCER COACH (BOYS) - PROVINE HIGH SCHOOL works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The HEAD SOCCER COACH (BOYS) - PROVINE HIGH SCHOOL at Jackson Public Sch. Dis. sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Jackson Public Sch. Dis., athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The HEAD SOCCER COACH (BOYS) - PROVINE HIGH SCHOOL encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The HEAD SOCCER COACH (BOYS) - PROVINE HIGH SCHOOL is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the HEAD SOCCER COACH (BOYS) - PROVINE HIGH SCHOOL position at Jackson Public Sch. Dis. is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.CompanyDescription: Jackson Public Sch. Dis. coaching jobs

Job.JobTitle: CALLAWAY HIGH SCHOOL ASST. BASKETBALL COACH,GIRLS (SY25-26)
Job.JobDescription:
The CALLAWAY HIGH SCHOOL ASST. BASKETBALL COACH,GIRLS (SY25-26) at Jackson Public Sch. Dis. is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the CALLAWAY HIGH SCHOOL ASST. BASKETBALL COACH,GIRLS (SY25-26) are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Jackson Public Sch. Dis. use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The CALLAWAY HIGH SCHOOL ASST. BASKETBALL COACH,GIRLS (SY25-26) recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the CALLAWAY HIGH SCHOOL ASST. BASKETBALL COACH,GIRLS (SY25-26) collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The CALLAWAY HIGH SCHOOL ASST. BASKETBALL COACH,GIRLS (SY25-26) maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Jackson Public Sch. Dis.. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

JOHNSTON COUNTY PUBLIC SCHOOLS JOB DESCRIPTION POSITION: Non-Faculty Coach LOCATION: Administrative and Auxiliary Services SALARY: JCPS Coach Supplement Scale REPORTS TO: Principal and School-Based Athletic Director JOB GOALS: An employee in this position will exhibit high moral character and execute scholastic based athletic principals. The Non-Faculty Coach will ensure safety of athletes while participating; teach good sportsmanship; establish a welcoming environment for all; and positively influence the students they work with. This Coach will work with administration, faculty, and staff to work towards the district’s goals regarding the whole child, academics, and athletics. QUALIFICATIONS AND ATTRIBUTES: Graduation from high school or equivalent required. Previous successful coaching experience preferred. Knowledgeable of all athletic policies approved by the Johnston County Schools’ Board of Education. Knowledgeable of existing system, state, and conference regulations. Ability and willingness to organize and supervise athletic team members. Must possess superior knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. Willingness to obtain a CDL to transport students on an approved activity bus to contests. Also, maintain certifications in order to do so, throughout tenure. Knowledgeable of the proper administrative line of command and the referral process for requests or grievances utilizing the proper chain of command. Knowledgeable of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility. Maintain or acquire all necessary coaching prerequisites annually or as they expire listed by NCDPI, NCHSAA, & Johnston County Public Schools. Promote the sport within the school through holding interest meetings and encouraging multisport athletic opportunities. Maintain all medical emergency information needed for each athlete, while also understanding emergency protocols if they were to arise. Promote the sport outside the school by being involved in the community, getting involved in coaching organizations, and attending required meetings. Provide quality and approved communication to the public. Maintain good public relations with members of the community, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Through the Athletic Director, prepare and release information to the public including schedules, tournaments, and results. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught in assigned duty. Provides training, rules, and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Assist with monitoring student’s athlete’s grades and conduct. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each athletic participant through regular attendance at games and practices. Follow policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies. Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries within 24 hours. Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Collaborate with the Athletic Director to inform and determine discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary, and contacts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assist the Head Coach in facilitating required pre-season meeting. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for sports events. Collaborate with the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method, or procedural changes. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates athletic program with maintenance and school employees. Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arranges for issuing, storing, and reconditioning or equipment and submits annual inventory and current records. Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices. Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on the general cleanliness of the facility. Is responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building. Instill in each player respect for equipment and school property, its care, and proper use. Perform such other duties as assigned by the Principal and or Superintendent of schools in keeping with Johnston County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. ADDITIONAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF HEAD COACH: Collaborate and participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to the type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment and other necessary resources. Operate and facilitate the athletic program within budget appropriations. Direct student managers, assistants, trainers, and statisticians. Communicate expectations to all supporting coaches and encourage their professional growth. Delegate specific duties and supervise the implementation of those duties. Collaborate with the Athletic Director to analyze the effectiveness of the coaching staff using an evaluation process. PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: Must be able to physically perform the basic life operational functions of climbing, balancing, stooping, kneeling, crouching, crawling, reaching, standing, walking, pushing, lifting, grasping, talking, hearing, and repetitive motions. Must be able to perform very heavy work exerting in excess of 100 pounds of force occasionally, and/or in excess of 50 pounds of force frequently and/or in excess of 25 pounds of force constantly to lift objects. Must possess the visual acuity to operate required equipment and motor vehicles, use measuring devices, and inspect work for accuracy, thoroughness, and adherence to work standards and instructions. Must possess the visual acuity to prepare data and statistics, work with figures, and records processes, operate a computer, and do extensive research and reading. DISCLAIMERS: The above statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this position. They are not intended to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and skills that may be required of the employees assigned to this position. This description may be revised by the supervisor, with HR review and approval, at any time. Johnston County Public Schools is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate in the employment or promotion of employees on the basis of gender, race, religion, age, national origin or disability.

Head Coach- Volleyball-Varsity Coach -Available 7/29/2026 Preferred NC Teaching License Resume Required. Please view the job description Nicole Clausi at nicoleclausi @ davidson.k12.nc.us Head Varsity Coach Preferred Valid NC Teaching Licensure Serve as liaison between the coaching staff and AD. Has a thorough knowledge of the rules and regulations of the NCHSAA, DCS, and Conference as they pertain to his/her sport, including verifying the eligibility of all staff members and student-athletes. Will provide a final roster to the AD. This includes following all DCS School Board Policies. Understands the proper administrative chain of command and refers all requests or grievances through the proper channels. Is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. Establishes the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by the staff. Delegates specific duties, supervises the implementation, and evaluates all assistants at the end of the season. Maintains discipline, mediates grievances, and works to increase morale. Assists the AD in scheduling and submitting transportation requests through Travel Tracker. Assists in the necessary preparation for scheduled home contests or practices and adheres to facility usage times. Communicates facility needs/repairs to the AD. Provides proper safeguards, including inspection, of all equipment. Monitors the grades and conduct of his/her student-athletes. Provides supervision, guidance, and safeguards for each student-athlete by his/her presence at all practices, and contests, while traveling, and when returning from off-campus events. Directs student managers, assistants, and statisticians. Determines discipline and procedures of due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contacts parents when a student-athlete is suspended, dropped, or becomes ineligible. Participates in the budgeting process with the AD by submitting needs for the next season. Is accountable for all equipment in his/her program and submits notification to the AD for any equipment lost, damaged, or not returned. Arranges for issuing, collecting, securing, reconditioning, and inventory of all equipment. Secures all doors, lights, windows, and locks and stores all equipment before leaving campus. Supervises locker room area before and after practice. Instills in each player respect for equipment and school property in its care and proper use. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Responsible for reporting scores to required outlets set forth by the NCHSAA. This duty may be delegated by the head coach. Guarantees that all district equipment including buses is used for official school business only and is operated safely. Performs other duties which may be assigned by the AD or Principal.

ATHLETIC COACH (to include Cheerleading Under general supervision, assist the board in the fair and just discharge of its obligation to students and staff through performance of the Head Coach role over and beyond the duties of a classroom teacher. Reports to the school principal and athletic director. SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Catawba County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff. Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by staff. Design conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program. Train and inform staff, encourage professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants. Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournament and special sport events. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees. Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility. Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method or procedural changes. Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Give constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct. Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant. Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies. Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours. Direct student managers, assistants, and statisticians. Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records. Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings. Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Catawba County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Catawba County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. Ability to communicate effectively with students, staff, parents, and the community. MINIMUM TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE As applicable, must meet requirements established by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, the Catawba County Board of Education, and school administration. Teacher license and employment as a teacher by the Catawba County Board of Education is strongly preferred. Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. DISCLAIMER The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees to this job.

POSITION DESCRIPTION POSITION TITLE Assistant Athletic Director SCHOOL/DEPARTMENT Assigned School SALARY Per the appropriate salary schedule FLSA STATUS Exempt REPORTS TO School Athletic Director SUPERVISES Coaches WORK WEEK SCHEDULE Monday – Friday; some evenings and weekends WORK HOURS 40 Minimum NUMBER OF MONTHS PER YEAR 10 POSITION PURPOSE: The Assistant Athletic Director assists the School Athletic Director regarding the provision and supervision of athletic programs at the assigned school. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS : KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Orange County Schools Board of Education Thorough knowledge of existing system, state, and conference regulations Thorough understanding of the proper administrative chain of command Thorough knowledge of the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques associated with the athletic program Thorough knowledge of state and federal requirements regarding physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility for athletic participation Thorough knowledge of training rules and any other unique regulations about the various sports in which athletes are engaged Thorough knowledge of guidance and safeguards in place for the safety of athletes to address medical injuries and emergencies Thorough knowledge of the process for initiating policies and procedures applicable to the athletic program EDUCATION, TRAINING, AND EXPERIENCE Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited institution in Physical Education or a related field Minimum of three (3) years of classroom experience as a Physical Education teacher at the middle or high school level Any equivalent combination of training and experience that provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities CERTIFICATION AND LICENSE REQUIREMENTS NC Teacher Certification in Physical Education Must possess a valid driver’s license issued by the NC Department of Motor Vehicles PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: Master’s Degree or additional credits beyond a Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited institution in a related field ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Responsible for the implementation by the entire staff of the sports program, according to the OCS Athletic Policy and Board of Education policies Implement existing system, state, and conference athletic regulations with fidelity Utilize the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests and grievances through proper channels Attend all required public/staff/departmental meetings Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by staff Design conferences, clinics, and staff meetings to ensure staff awareness of the overall program as needed Encourage staff professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy Delegate specific duties, supervise implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and help evaluate staff as needed Maintain discipline, address grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation Assist the Athletic Director with scheduling, providing transportation, and communicating requirements for tournaments and special sporting events Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices, and adhere to scheduled facility times Coordinate programs with maintenance and school employees Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility Provide proper safeguards for the maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites Advise the Athletic Director and Athletic Supervisor regarding any concerns and recommend policy, method, or procedural changes Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant Monitor and give constant attention to student-athletes' grades and conduct Must be present at all practices and games Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant Share responsibilities for upkeep and regular care of facilities and fields Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies Complete and submit proper paperwork regarding all disabling athletic injuries to the athletic office within 24 hours of the occurrence Determine discipline by school system policies Communicate procedures regarding due process entitlements when the enforcement of discipline is necessary Contact parents when a student is dropped from a team or becomes ineligible to participate Assist athletes with their college or advanced educational selections Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season Recommend guidelines regarding type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment Assist with operating within budget appropriations Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing and/or storing Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices; authorize who may enter Issue and/ or requisition equipment Limit student-athlete presence to authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games Check on the general cleanliness of the facility, and be responsible for the cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building if custodians are not on duty Instill in each player a respect for each other, equipment, and school property, emphasizing care and proper use of equipment Help organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for the pre-season meeting Promote the sport within the school by recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program Promote the sport outside the school via news media, Little League programs, or in any other feasible manner Maintain good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and spectators Present information to the news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results, if needed Perform other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director, Athletic Supervisor, and/or School Principal WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS Substantial standing, walking, moving, climbing, carrying, bending, kneeling, crawling, reaching, handling, pushing, and pulling. Work is considered moderate physical work requiring the exertion of up to 50 pounds of force. WORK ENVIRONMENT Employees in this position are required to work in indoor and outdoor environments and come into direct contact with OCS staff, students, and the public. JOB DESCRIPTION GENERAL STATEMENT OF JOB An employee in this position will be a personal example of high moral character and superior teaching standards. The Assistant Wrestling Coach will teach good sportsmanship as the number one priority on the athletic field. This Coach will work with the faculty and staff to support player academic and moral success. QUALIFICATIONS Must be licensed or be eligible to receive a NC Teaching License with experience as a wrestling coach or extensiveknowledge in this area. OR Must have experience as a wrestling coach or extensive knowledge in this area to be eligible to serve as a non-faculty coach.
