The Assistant Outdoor Track Coach at Lexington City is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Assistant Outdoor Track Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Lexington City use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Assistant Outdoor Track Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Assistant Outdoor Track Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Assistant Outdoor Track Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Lexington City. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

The Assistant Baseball Coach at Lexington City is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Assistant Baseball Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Lexington City, the Assistant Baseball Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Assistant Baseball Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Lexington City well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Assistant Baseball Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

JOB TITLE: Head Football Coach REPORTS TO: Athletic Director and Principal SALARY: Based on State and Local Salary Schedule SUMMARY: The job of Head Football Coach was established for the purpose to assist the board in the fair and just discharge its obligation to students and staff through performance of the Head Coach role over and beyond the duties of a classroom teacher. RESPONSIBILITIES & DUTIES: Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Caswell County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff. Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Design conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program Train and inform staff, encourage professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase cooperation and morale Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method or procedural changes Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings Provide training rules and any other unique regulations o the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, it’s care and proper use Give constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours Direct student managers, assistants and statisticians Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible Assist athletes in the college or advanced educational selection Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans Present information to the news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Caswell County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes MINIMUM EDUCATION, EXPERIENCE AND REQUIREMENTS: As applicable, must meet requirements established by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, the Caswell County Board of Education, and school administration Teacher license and employment as a teacher by the Caswell County Board of Education is strongly preferred Applicant may be asked to hold a 10-month teaching position, plus one additional month for coaching duties Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES : Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Caswell County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to stand, walk, sit, talk and listen. The employee is occasionally required to use hands to fingers, handle, or feel: reach with hands and arms; and stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 15 pounds or more at times.

Athletic Coach The coach should understand his/her sport and be familiar with all rules, courtesies, and procedures of the activity. The coach should know his/her students and be an effective leader. He/she should be concerned with winning gracefully and, when losing, accept the loss with poise and a realization that an educational experience probably has been achieved. Specific Duties Teach the physical skills needed to play specific sport. Know and follow all rules and regulations governing the s Provide fair and equal opportunities for all candidate Plan and supervise practice sessions in keeping with the age and maturity of the play Meet with athletic director to prepare a tentative schedule of game Establish rules for the proper use and care of athletic equipmen Prepare and present to the athletic director a list of students desiring to participate so that eligibility can be determ Check and monitor team members' academic Maintain an inventory of all equipment and supplies. Submit to the athletic director at the end of the season a list of any equipment and/or uniforms needed for the upcoming y Submit a budget request to athletic director Collect uniforms and equipment at the end of the sport season and account for same. Perform other duties relative to coaching as assigned by the principal and the athletic director. Follow all fiscal control act procedures for collected monies. Adhered to athletic and coach handbook as well as state and conference regulations. Requirements: Leadership skills must encourage academic excellence, development of positive community relationships, and communication with a diverse population of students, staff, school and community. Must possess or can obtain a North Carolina Teaching Licensing through North Carolina Department of Public Instruction. Must possess or can obtain a North Carolina CDL license, CPR & AED certifications and complete the NFHS Required Course work within 30 days of hire.

Job.JobTitle: Boy's Assistant Lacrosse Athletic Coach | West Forsyth High School
Job.JobDescription: Assistant Coach: Boys Lacrosse Coach This is a part-time coaching position only. Assistant Lacrosse coaching supplement is based on the athletic special supplement schedule for 3.5 months in accordance with coaching experience. Supplement range for Assistant Lacrosse Coach is $311.00 to $584.00 monthly. Additional coaching duties (if any) with additional supplement pay will be assigned by the Principal and/or Athletic Director. After applying, please also send resume to Mike Pennington, Athletics Director, West Forsyth High School, 1735 Lewisville Clemmons Road, Clemmons, NC 27012, mpennington@wsfcs.k12.nc.us Job Qualifications: Successful high school and/or college lacrosse experience, or other relevant experience. Hold or be eligible to hold a NC commercial drivers license to operate a school activity bus preferred. Will be subject to periodic US Dept. of Transportation random controlled substance screenings. When applying, please include all prior experience.

ORANGE COUNTY SCHOOLS POSITION TITLE Assistant Coach SCHOOL/DEPARTMENT Assigned school/ Assigned sport SALARY Per the appropriate salary schedule FLSA STATUS Exempt REPORTS TO Head Coach, Athletic Director SUPERVISES None WORK WEEK SCHEDULE Monday – Friday; evenings and some weekends WORK HOURS As dictated by the sport and season NUMBER OF MONTHS PER YEAR As dictated by the sport and season POSITION PURPOSE: The Assistant Coach is responsible for providing appropriate coaching guidance to student-athletes related to a particular sport for the duration of the sports season. As part of the Athletic Program, at the assigned school, the Assistant Coach is responsible for teaching and modeling good sportsmanship behaviors, appropriate techniques, and skills related to the particular sport, ensuring safe practices are implemented to protect the students from physical injuries, and for serving as good role models for the students, the school and the school system. KNOWLEDGE OF POSITION: • Thorough knowledge of the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques associated with the athletic program • Thorough knowledge of state and federal requirements regarding physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility for athletic participation • Thorough knowledge of training rules and any other unique regulations about the various sports in which athletes are engaged • Thorough knowledge of guidance and safeguards in place for the safety of athletes to address medical injuries and emergencies • Thorough knowledge of the process for initiating policies and procedures applicable to the athletic program EDUCATION, TRAINING, AND EXPERIENCE • High School Diploma or GED • Additional course work and/or training in a particular sport • Any equivalent combination of training and experience that provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: • Associate’s Degree or Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited institution in Physical Education or related field • Minimum of three (3) years of coaching experience at the middle or high school level • NC Certification as Teacher, preferably in Physical Education CERTIFICATION AND LICENSE REQUIREMENTS • Must possess a valid driver’s license issued by the NC Department of Motor Vehicles ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Implement, interpret and enforce consistently existing school system, state, and conference regulations 2. Refer all requests of grievances through proper channels 3. Attend all public/staff/departmental meetings for which attendance is required 4. Maintain discipline and work to increase morale and cooperation within the school sports program 5. Assist the Head Coach with scheduling, and providing transportation to tournaments and special sports events 6. Assist with the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times 7. Assist with sporting events and practices with maintenance and school employees 8. Assist with proper safeguard for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites 9. Assist with training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant 10. Attend all practices and games 11. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant while traveling 12. Assist with directing student managers and statisticians on the respective team 13. Assist the Head Coach when determining discipline, delineating procedure concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary, and contacting parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible to participate 14. Accountable to the Head Coach for all equipment 15. Assist with the collection of the cost of any equipment lost or not returned 16. Assist with issuing and storage of equipment and submit an annual inventory and current records to the Head Coach 17. Recommend program area budgetary items for subsequent years to the Head Coach 18. Assist with monitoring and authorizing entrance to equipment rooms and coaches’ offices 19. Assist with examining locker rooms for general cleanliness before and after practices and games 20. Share responsibilities for upkeep and regular care of facilities and fields 21. Assist with ensuring cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment 22. Assist in securing all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building if custodians are not on duty 23. Assist with instilling in each player respect for each other, athletic equipment, and school property, including its care and proper use 24. Assist the Head Coach in carrying out their responsibilities 25. Assist with instructing the team members regarding changes in the rules and teaching fundamentals of the sport as outlined by the Head Coach 26. Assist with maintaining a record of team statistics and requirements for lettering (for Head Coach) 27. Work within the basic framework and philosophy of the Head Coach of that sport 28. Attend all staff meetings and carry out scouting assignments as outlined by the Head Coach 29. Arrive early enough before practice, contests, and meetings to assist with preparation adequately and remain long enough afterward to help players with problems and/or be involved in staff discussions 30. Assist with the planning and implementation of both in-season and out-of-season conditioning and weight programs 31. Strive to improve skills by attending clinics and using resources made available by the Head Coach 32. Attend most of the contests, when possible, of other teams in the program 33. Perform other duties as assigned by the Head Coach or Athletic Director DISCLAIMER: The above statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this position. They are not intended to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and skills that may be required of the employees assigned to this position. This description may be revised by the supervisor, with HR review and approval, at any time. WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS Depending on the sport, work may be considered light to heavy requiring the exertion of up to 100 pounds of force. Position may require a lot of standing, walking, kneeling, squatting, pushing, pulling, and running. WORK ENVIRONMENT Employees in this position are required to work in indoor and outdoor environments, with possible exposure to excessive heat and cold, and come into direct contact with OCS staff, students, and the public.

Coach TERM OF EMPLOYMENT: Pre-season skill development and conditioning; regular season coaching; non exempt position. QUALIFICATIONS: Knowlege and expierence in applied sport. DUTIES: Responsible for conducting daily practice sessions, home contests as well as travel with team to away contests, monitoring academic progress, interacting with parents, media, community, and promoting sportsmanship. BENEFITS: No Benefits Available APPLICATION DEADLINE: Applications will be accepted until position is filled. **The Jackson County School System is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate against any person in hiring, tenure, or promotion on the basis of sex, race, color, religion, national origin, age or handicaps in any of its education or employment programs or activities. Assignments are made in accordance with the needs of the school system and are subject to change.**

ATHLETIC COACH (to include Cheerleading Under general supervision, assist the board in the fair and just discharge of its obligation to students and staff through performance of the Head Coach role over and beyond the duties of a classroom teacher. Reports to the school principal and athletic director. SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Catawba County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff. Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by staff. Design conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program. Train and inform staff, encourage professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants. Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournament and special sport events. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees. Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility. Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method or procedural changes. Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Give constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct. Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant. Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies. Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours. Direct student managers, assistants, and statisticians. Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records. Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings. Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Catawba County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Catawba County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. Ability to communicate effectively with students, staff, parents, and the community. MINIMUM TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE As applicable, must meet requirements established by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, the Catawba County Board of Education, and school administration. Teacher license and employment as a teacher by the Catawba County Board of Education is strongly preferred. Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. DISCLAIMER The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees to this job.

Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment needed Responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Account for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment Submit annual inventory and current records Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices; clarify and authorize who may enter, issue, or requisition equipment Restrict athletes to authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on the general cleanliness of the facility. Ensure cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment Share responsibilities for upkeep and regular care of facilities and fields Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building if custodians are not on duty Instill in each player respect for each other, athletic equipment, and school property, including its care and proper use Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings Promote the sport within the school by recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program Promote the sport outside the school through news media, Little League programs, or in any other feasible manner Work with the Athletic Director and Chief Communications Officer to ensure the quality, effectiveness, and validity of any oral or written release to local media Maintain excellent public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results Perform other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or Principal WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS Depending on the sport, work may be considered light to heavy requiring the exertion of up to 100 pounds of force. Position may require a lot of standing, walking, kneeling, squatting, pushing, pulling, and running. WORK ENVIRONMENT Employees in this position are required to work in indoor and outdoor environments, with possible exposure to excessive heat and cold, and come into direct contact with OCS staff, students, and the public. JOB DESCRIPTION GENERAL STATEMENT OF JOB An employee in this position will be a personal example of high moral character and superior teaching standards. The Head JV Baseball Coach will teach good sportsmanship as the number one priority on the athletic field. This Coach will work with the faculty and staff to support player academic and moral success. QUALIFICATIONS NC State Teachers Salary Schedule Local Athletic Supplement (Based on degree and experience) AND/OR Head JV Baseball Coach Plus Local Teacher Supplement $739.20 - $1593.90 per season (Based on exp. as a baseball coach) SALARY INFORMATION Must be licensed or be eligible to receive a NC Teaching License with experience as a baseball coach or extensive knowledge in this area. OR Must have experience as a baseball coach or extensive knowledge in this area to be eligible to serve as a non-faculty coach. This role supports athletic performance, physical development, and overall student wellness while aligning with school, district, and state athletic guidelines. The High School Defensive Coordinator is responsible for leading and managing all aspects of the defensive side of the football program. This position plays a vital role in developing student-athletes both on and off the field by fostering discipline, accountability, leadership and teamwork. The Defensive Coordinator works closely with the Head Coach and coaching staff to build a competitive, well prepared, and fundamentally sound defense that reflects the values and standards of Lexington Senior High School. Qualifications - Previous high school or collegiate football coaching experience. Defense preferred. - Strong Knowledge of defensive schemes, coverages, and front alignments. - Ability to teach and communicate effectively with student-athletes. - Demonstrate leadership skills and ability to build positive relationships. - Commitment to developing student-athletes academically, athletically and socially - Ability to work collaboratively with staff, administration, parents, and community stakeholders. Preferred Qualifications - Prior Defensive Coordinator or Head Coaching Experience - Experience using film breakdown and scouting software (Hudl) - Teaching license or eligibility for employment with the school district.

The High School Offensive Coordinator is responsible for leading and managing all aspects of the offensive side of the football program. This position plays a vital role in developing student-athletes both on and off the field by fostering discipline, accountability, leadership and teamwork. The Offensive Coordinator works closely with the Head Coach and coaching staff to build a competitive, well-prepared, and fundamentally sound offense that reflects the values and standards of Lexington Senior High School. Qualifications -Previous high school or collegiate football coaching experience (offense preferred) - Strong Knowledge of offensive schemes, formations and situational football. - Ability to teach and communicate effectively with student-athletes. - Demonstrate Leadership skills and ability to build positive relationships. - Commitment to developing student-athletes academically, athletically and socially. - Ability to work collaboratively with staff, administration, parents and community stakeholders. Preferred Qualifications -Prior Offensive Coordinator or Head Coaching Experience - Experience using film breakdown and scouting software (Hudl) - Teaching License or eligibilty for employment within the school district.

The High School Assistant Football Coach supports the Head Coach in all aspects of the football program, contributing to the development of the student-athletes on and off the field. The position plays a critical role in skill instruction, practice organizaton, game preparation, and fostering a culture of discipline, sportsmanship and accoutabilty. The Assistant Coach is expected to serve as a positive role model and uphold the values and standards of Lexington Senior High School. Qualifications - Previous playing or coaching experience at the High School or Collegiate level. - Strong Knowledge of Football Fundamentals, rules and strategies - Ability to motivate and mentor high school students Preffered to have a teaching license or eligibilty of employment with the school district.

The Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) role at Lincoln Public Sch. is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Lincoln Public Sch., teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Lincoln Public Sch., the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) at Lincoln Public Sch. is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

North Star High School Varsity Head Dance Coach Requires Teacher and/or coach who holds a Nebraska Department of Education Teacher or Administrative Certificate or a Nebraska Department of Education Special Services Coaching Permit. Lincoln North Star High School is seeking a varsity head coach to provide qualified leadership for the Dance Team. Good communication and organizational skills are required as well as the ability to develop positive, productive relationships with student-athletes, parents, and the community. Must be able to provide quality instruction of fundamental skills as well as have a comprehensive knowledge of the sport of Dance. The successful candidate must have the ability to lead and enhance the ongoing development, improvement, and participation in the program. Be responsible for inspecting and maintaining the inventory and equipment; reporting contest results; and must know, implement, and enforce national, state, and local athletic policies. This position is subject to a veterans preference. Job Description: Coach

Lincoln Northeast Rockets: Reserve Girls Tennis The applicant must show strong leadership skills, works well with students and adults, and values hard work and is committed to the activity and has knowledge of the sport of tennis. The applicant must work well with others and is willing to listen and adapt to plans from the Varsity level. This position will focus on teaching basics and cultivating a growth mindset for players who are fairly new to the sport. This position will require acting as the head coach at Reserve level matches, and to help train players to progress through the levels of high school tennis. Requires certified teacher or coach who holds a Nebraska Department of Education Teacher or Administrative Certificate or a Nebraska Department of Education Special Services This position also require NFHS Certifications in Concusion Awareness, Sudden Cardiac Arrest, and Heat Illness. Also this position requires CPR Certification. These certfications may be completed after the hiring is confirmed. This position is subject to a veterans preference. Job Description: Coach

Lincoln Northwest High School is seeking a dynamic individual as an assistant boys soccer coach to provide support for athletes in the Lincoln Northwest Boys Soccer program. Excellent communication and organizational skills are required as well as the desire and ability to develop positive relationships with student-participants, families, and the LPS community. Must have comprehensive knowledge and experience with the sport of soccer to help develop the school’s program. LPS assistant coaches are expected to follow the guidance of their head coaches in organizing and leading practices, regular season and district contests and performances (not to exceed NSAA/LPS allowances); facilitate, manage and provide oversight of the program; maintain required certification and requirements of the position; and lead their teams in NSAA state championship preparation and performances if the team or individuals qualify. LPS has a long tradition of academic, athletic and activity excellence. Belief in the value of educational-based athletics is critical. Requires teacher and/or coach who holds a Nebraska Department of Education Teacher or Administrative Certificate or a Nebraska Department of Education Special Services Coaching Permit. Knowledge and understanding of NSAA and LPS policies and bylaws is required. This position is subject to a veterans preference. Job Description: Coach

Job.JobTitle: High School Assistant Track and Field Coach (2026-2027)
Job.JobDescription: BENNINGTON PUBLIC SCHOOLS Position Description Position Title: Assistant High School Track and Field Coach (2026-2027) JOB DESCRIPTION JOB TITLE: Assistant High School Track and Field Coach - Bennington High School REPORTS TO: Activities Director TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Part-Time I. ACCOUNTABILITY OBJECTIVES: To instruct athletes in fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. The student shall also receive instruction that will end to the formation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, self-confidence, resilience, and cooperation with teammates. Regular, dependable, in-person attendance is an essential function of the job. II. POSITION CHARACTERISTICS: Recruited by: Athletic Director/Principal Wages: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Vacation: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Holidays: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Sick Leave: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Work Day: Hours to be determined by Activities Director/Principal Pay Day: As determined by Negotiated Agreement. III. POSITION RELATIONSHIPS: Reports to Activities Director Coordinates with: Activities Director, Building Principal, Custodians, and Transportation Supervisor. IV. POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES: Be responsible for the development and formulation of his/her program in grades 9-12 Oversee a systematic skill development program for all athletes in the program Recommend assistant coaches and coach’s aides and officials to the Activities Director Essential Functions The essential functions of the position include: regular, dependable attendance on the job; the ability to perform the identified tasks and to possess and utilize the identified knowledge, skills and abilities to perform the identified work activities; and, the ability to perform the identified physical requirements as outlined in working environment. Working Environment The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is required to lift and pull or push articles up to 50 pounds. He/she sits most of the time but must walk frequently and, at times, stand. The employee is required to reach with his/her hands and continuously to repeat the same hand, arm, or finger motion many times as in typing. Manual and finger dexterity are essential to the job. He/she is constantly working to meet deadlines with severe time restraints. He/she must see and read fine print such as typed or handwritten materials and have good depth perception (fine muscle control). The employee must, also, be able to hear conversation in a noisy environment and to communicate clearly and concisely through speech. __ The information contained in this job description is for compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and is not an exhaustive list of the duties performed for this position. Additional duties are performed by the individuals currently holding this position and additional duties may be assigned. EOE

Job.JobTitle: Activities - Assistant Softball Coach - High School (1 year only)
Job.JobDescription:
The Activities - Assistant Softball Coach - High School (1 year only) position at Fremont Public Sch. is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Activities - Assistant Softball Coach - High School (1 year only) works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Activities - Assistant Softball Coach - High School (1 year only) at Fremont Public Sch. sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Fremont Public Sch., athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Activities - Assistant Softball Coach - High School (1 year only) encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Activities - Assistant Softball Coach - High School (1 year only) is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Activities - Assistant Softball Coach - High School (1 year only) position at Fremont Public Sch. is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Assumes responsibility for the entire program which includes: Coaching and supervising the program. Implements district athletic department policies. Works with the MSD Athletic Director and Principal Communicating school game schedule. Communication of travel for out-of-town trips. Verification of student physical examination/Family ID requirements of the high school. Remains with students and supervises them while traveling to away competitions. Cares for athletes and injured athletes. Cares for, maintains and oversees the storage of all equipment and uniforms. Maintains a positive staff relationship with other coaches. Performs other duties as assigned by the MSD Athletic Director and Principal Communicate with parents to inform them about the program as well as your expectations of them and the players. Leads by example Apply and enforce student discipline during athletic contests, practice sessions, and while on trips off school property in accordance with Student Code of Conduct and student handbook.

The Elementary Boys Basketball Coach position at Manchester School Dist. provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Elementary Boys Basketball Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Manchester School Dist., the Elementary Boys Basketball Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Elementary Boys Basketball Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Elementary Boys Basketball Coach at Manchester School Dist. is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Assumes responsibility for the program which includes: Coaching the program. Implements district athletic department policies. Communicating the game schedule. Verification of student physical examination/Family ID requirements of the high school. Remains with students and supervises them while traveling to away competitions. Cares for athletes and injured athletes. Cares for, maintains and oversees the storage of all equipment and uniforms. Maintains a positive staff relationship with other coaches. Performs other duties as assigned by the MSD Athletic Director/Athletic Coordinator and Principal. Leads by example regarding promotion of sportsmanship. Promotes the entire program by encouraging participation of players of the upcoming season. Apply and enforce student discipline during athletic contests, practice sessions, and while on trips off school property in accordance with Student Code of Conduct and student handbook.

Job.JobTitle: High School Girls Lacrosse Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription: Assists with responsibility for the entire program which includes: Coaching the varsity squad and supervising JV & assistant coaches within the program. Giving guidance to the high school junior varsity program. Implements district athletic department policies. Assists in the selection and evaluation of Junior Varsity and assistant coaching staff. Works with the MSD Athletic Director, Athletic Coordinator and Principal Communicating high school game schedule. Communication of travel for out-of-town trips. Verification of student physical examination/Family ID requirements of the high school. Remains with students and supervises them while traveling to away competitions. Cares for athletes and injured athletes. Cares for, maintains and oversees the storage of all equipment and uniforms. Prepares award lists and season records. Attends all state meetings where the program is involved and operates within the policies of the MSD. Keeps informed on current trends in your sport and attends and participates in clinics in your sport. Maintains active membership in related professional organizations. Maintains a positive staff relationship with other coaches. Performs other duties as assigned by the MSD Athletic Director/Athletic Coordinator and Principal to inform them about the program as well as your expectations of them and the players. Communicates with players about personal development during the season as well as the off-season. Holds meetings with parents regarding sportsmanship. Promotes the high school program by encouraging participation of players of the upcoming season. Apply and enforce student discipline during athletic contests, practice sessions, and while on trips off school property in accordance with Student Code of Conduct and student handbook.
